EAS.name: Enrollment Application System
EAS.description: The Enrollment Application System (EAS) package supports the mandate for collection of Long Term Care (LTC) copayments, as required by Public Law 106-117.  In addition to the LTC functionality, the EAS package provides support for  Health Level 7 (HL7) for processing of HL7 messages between VistA, the enterprise Enrollment System (ES) and data matching with the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) / Social Security Administration (SSA)

DG.name: Registration
DG.description: The ADT module provides a comprehensive range of software dedicated to the support of administrative functions related to patient admission, discharge, transfer, and registration
rpcs: DG CHK MEANS TEST DIV DISPLAY
DG CHK PAT MEANS TEST REQUIRED

DGQE.name: Registration
DGQE.description: The ADT/Registration module provides a comprehensive range of software dedicated to the support of administrative functions related to patient admission, discharge, transfer, and registration

DPT.name: Registration
DPT.description: The ADT/Registration module provides a comprehensive range of software dedicated to the support of administrative functions related to patient admission, discharge, transfer, and registration

VA.name: Registration
VA.description: The ADT/Registration module provides a comprehensive range of software dedicated to the support of administrative functions related to patient admission, discharge, transfer, and registration

VIC.name: Registration
VIC.description: The ADT/Registration module provides a comprehensive range of software dedicated to the support of administrative functions related to patient admission, discharge, transfer, and registration

QAO.name: Occurrence Screen
QAO.description: This module supports VHA policy by identifying patients' clinical events requiring follow-up review

IBQ.name: Utilization Management Rollup
IBQ.description: This module is used at the local facility for rollup of Utilization Management Rollup data to the National Database

QAQ.name: Quality Assurance Integration
QAQ.description: The Quality Management Integration Module, (previously Quality Assurance Integration) contains utilities that are common to some or all of the QM software packages

QAP.name: Survey Generator
QAP.description: This module allows creation and maintenance of computerized survey forms

CRHD.name: Shift Handoff Tool
CRHD.description: The module standardizes information exchanged between clinicians as they transfer patient care responsibilities incidental to changes of shifts

ECT.name: Interim Management Support
ECT.description: No documentation exist. Menu options include: VAMC space management, equipment, workload, quality assurance, etc.

OOPS.name: Asists
OOPS.description: The Auto Safety Incident Surv Track System (ASISTS) module was designed to manage the data from all employee accidents, create a Report of Accident (VA Form 2162) from the data, and produce both the Office of Worker's Compensation Programs Form CA-1 (Instructions for Completing Employee's Notice of Traumatic Injury and Claim for Continuation of Pay/Compensation) and Form CA-2 (Federal Employee's Notice of Occupational Disease and Claim for Compensation)
rpcs: OOPS GET PRT ACC STATUS RPT

ABSV.name: Voluntary Timekeeping
ABSV.description: The site-based Voluntary Timekeeping module has been replaced by the national-level Voluntary Service System (VSS) application; it is used to track and manage the hours of service contributed by volunteers and volunteer organizations at VA facilities

SIS.name: Sea Island Systems Registration
SIS.description: HL7 Interface software to integrate with third party ADT packages

QAN.name: Incident Reporting
QAN.description: The Incident Reporting module supports VHA policy by compiling data on patient incidents.  It organizes the data into defined categories for reporting and tracking at medical facility level and for transmission to the National Quality Assurance Database for Headquarters review and tracking

LBR.name: Library
LBR.description: P,atch LBR*2.5*15 retired the Library (LBR) version 2.5 package.  All national components associated with the Library package were removed as of compliance date, May 3, 2018

LBRS.name: Library
LBRS.description: P,atch LBR*2.5*15 retired the Library (LBR) version 2.5 package.  All national components associated with the Library package were removed as of compliance date, May 3, 2018

WV.name: Womens Health
WV.description: The module is a patient management tool for tracking the breast and gynecologic treatment needs of women, the procedures they receive, and the communications between healthcare staff and women regarding their treatment and follow-up. The application also provides some program-wide epidemiological reports which are of use to clinicians and program administrators

ONC.name: Oncology
ONC.description: The module automates the tumor registry and supports tumor registrars in abstracting cancer cases, following up on cancer patients and producing the Hospital Annual Report. Functions are grouped according to order of use:  Case Finding and Suspense; Abstracting, Printing and Quality Management; Follow-up; Registry Lists; Annual Reports; Statistical Reports; and Utilities

ROR.name: Clinical Case Registries
ROR.description: The Clinical Case Registries (CCR) application obtains demographic and clinical data on VHA patients with specific clinical conditions.  CCR is designed to search and provide reports on patient data in multiple registries.  This aides clinical staff in supporting a variety of clinical conditions or disease states in VHA patients

IMR.name: Clinical Case Registries
IMR.description: The Clinical Case Registries (CCR) application obtains demographic and clinical data on VHA patients with specific clinical conditions.  CCR is designed to search and provide reports on patient data in multiple registries.  This aides clinical staff in supporting a variety of clinical conditions or disease states in VHA patients

MMRS.name: Methicillin Resistant Staph Aurerus Initiative Reports
MMRS.description: The MRSA Program Tools (MRSA-PT) application provides a method to extract data related to MRSA Nares screening, clinical cultures, and patient movements within the selected facility

GMR.name: General Medical Record - Vitals
GMR.description: The module is designed to store, in the patient's electronic health record, all vital signs and various measurements associated with a patient's hospital stay or outpatient clinic visit. Data entered can be accessed by several VistA applications (e.g., Health Summary) that interface with the Vitals/Measurements application

XUS.name: Kernel
XUS.description: Kernel provides a portability layer between the underlying operating system and application code
rpcs: XUS KAAJEE GET USER VIA PROXY
XUS MVI NEW PERSON BULK GET

XUSRB.name: Kernel
XUSRB.description: Kernel provides a portability layer between the underlying operating system and application code
rpcs: XUS KAAJEE GET USER VIA PROXY
XUS MVI NEW PERSON BULK GET

DDR.name: VA FileMan
DDR.description: VA FileMan is the VistA database management system (DBMS).  It runs in any American National Standards Institute (ANSI) environment.  The majority of VHA clinical data is stored in VA FileMan files and is retrieved and accessed through VA FileMan Application Program Interfaces (API) and user interfaces

VE.name: Order Entry Results Reporting
VE.description: The module Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) enables you to enter, review, and continuously update allinformation connected with any patient
rpcs: ORCDLR2 CHECK ALL LC TO WC
ORCDLR2 CHECK ONE LC TO WC

rpcs.name: XWB EGCHO STRING
rpcs.description: This RPC receives a string which will be sent right back to the client. It exists for support of EGcho - Broker demonstration program. to run d ECHO^XWBZ1(something)

rpcs.name: XWB EGCHO LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC brings back a small list of elements to the client.  It exists for support of EGcho - Broker demonstration program.

rpcs.name: XWB EGCHO BIG LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC brings back a lot of meaningless data to the client.  It exists for support of EGcho - Broker demonstration program.

rpcs.name: XWB EGCHO SORT LIST
rpcs.description: Sorts a given numeric array, starting from HI or LO It exists for support of EGcho - Broker demonstration program.
rpcs.returnDescription: Sorted array

rpcs.name: XWB EGCHO MEMO
rpcs.description: This RPC accepts text from a client which it will send right back to the client.  It exists for support of EGcho - Broker demonstration program.

rpcs.name: XWB RPC LIST
rpcs.description: Returns a list of remote procedures from the REMOTE PROCEDURE file.

rpcs.name: XWB GET VARIABLE VALUE
rpcs.description: This RPC accepts the name of a variable which will be evaluated and its Integration Agreement (IA) #1629 value returned to the server.  For example, this RPC may be called with a parameter variable like DUZ which will be returned as 123456.  It should NOT be used to return the value of anything other than a variable. For example, the RPC should not attempt to return the value of a global or function call, as these are unsupported uses of the RPC and are not guaranteed to work consistently.

rpcs.name: XUS SIGNON SETUP
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #1632 - API ICR #4054 Establishes the environment necessary for VistA sign-on.
rpcs.returnDescription: RET(0)=server name RET(1)=volume RET(2)=uci RET(3)=device RET(4)=# attempts RET(5)=skip signon-screen RET(6)=Domain Name RET(7)=production (0=no, 1=yes)

rpcs.name: XUS SEND KEYS
rpcs.description: Returns an array of strings that are used in the hashing algorithm. The strings that are returned are picked up from Z^XUSRB.

rpcs.name: XUS AV CODE
rpcs.description: This API checks if a ACCESS/VERIFY code pair is valid. R(5+n)=message text. It returns an array of values  R(0)=DUZ if sign-on was OK, zero if not OK.', "R(1)=(0=OK, 1,2...=Can't sign-on for some reason).", 'R(2)=verify needs changing. R(3)=Message. R(4)=0 R(5)=count of the number of lines of text, zero if none.

rpcs.name: XUS INTRO MSG
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the INTRO message from the KERNEL SYSTEM PARAMETERS file.

rpcs.name: XWB CREATE CONTEXT
rpcs.description: Establishes context on the server, which will be checked by the Broker before The user has no access to that option  The option is temporarily out of order  An application can switch from one context to another as often as it needs to. Each time a context is created the previous context is overwritten.  For more information on creating a context and the overall Broker security see Broker on-line help documentation.  executing any other remote procedure.  Since context is nothing more than a Inte

rpcs.name: SC LISTER
rpcs.description: Per LIST^DIC(), returns the starting location, number of records to retrieve, file to retrieve them from, and index to use.

rpcs.name: SC FILER
rpcs.description: Generic call to file edits into FM file.
rpcs.returnDescription: If file update is successful, then a 1 is returned. A 0 is returned if unsuccessful.

rpcs.name: SC DELETE ENTRY
rpcs.description: Allows file entry deletion via FileMan ^DIK call.
rpcs.returnDescription: Indicates whether delete operation was successful.

rpcs.name: SC FIND
rpcs.description: Per FIND^DIC, find records in a file.

rpcs.name: SC FILE NUMBER
rpcs.description: Broker callback to get file number.

rpcs.name: SC GLOBAL NODE
rpcs.description: Call to pass back a global node.

rpcs.name: SC GETS ENTRY DATA
rpcs.description: Calls database server at GETS^DIQ.

rpcs.name: SC LOCK/UNLOCK NODE
rpcs.description: Allow locking/unlocking of M global node.
rpcs.returnDescription: Indicates whether lock operation was successful.

rpcs.name: SC VALIDATOR
rpcs.description: This function allows the application to validate user input to a field before filing data. The call uses the database server VAL^DIE call.
rpcs.returnDescription: This call passes back information in the [data

rpcs.name: SC GLOBAL NODE COUNT
rpcs.description: Returns the number of entries found in the global node at the ROOT,XREF, VALUE.  If 0 is returned, then there were no values at that node. Pass in ("ROOT"), ("XREF"), ("VALUE")
rpcs.returnDescription: The number of entries found in the global node at the xref and value.

rpcs.name: SC PRTP
rpcs.description: Returns the currently active staff member for a position.  Send in Position Ien and date range, returns name and ien from New Person file. SC("IEN")=ien SC("BEGIN")=begining date for check SC("END")=ending date for check

rpcs.name: SC MAILMAN
rpcs.description: Sends Mailman message from information sent from client

rpcs.name: SC NEW HISTORY OK
rpcs.description: This call passes back whether the date for a new history entry is valid or not.  Primarily for files 404.52, 404.58, 404.59.  Returns a 1 if ok, or a 0 if not.

rpcs.name: SC CHANGE HISTORY OK
rpcs.description: This call returns a 1 if changing an exisiting date for a History entry is ok, or a 0 if the date cannot be changed.  Primary history files are 404.52, 404.58, 404.59

rpcs.name: SC INACTIVATE ENTRY
rpcs.description: This call returns a 1 if the entry can be inactivated, or a 0 if the entry cannot.  Used primarily for history files.

rpcs.name: SC DELETE HISTORY
rpcs.description: This call returns a 1 if the entry can be deleted, a 0 if it cannot be deleted.  Restricted to files 404.52, 404.58, 404.59 history files at present.

rpcs.name: SC HISTORY STATUS OK
rpcs.description: This call returns a 1 if the status of a new history entry is ok, a 0 if it is not.  Currently restricted to files, 404.52, 404.58, 404.59

rpcs.name: SC MEAN TEST
rpcs.description: Returns mean test data for patient from API call LST^DGMTU
rpcs.returnDescription: Mean test data in string format

rpcs.name: SC TEAM LIST
rpcs.description: Returns a list of teams the patient is currently assigned to.

rpcs.name: SC PATIENT LOOKUP
rpcs.description: Patient lookup.  This is intended as a temporary RPC until a VA or FileMan component is available.  Does a Multiple index lookup on the PATIENT file (#2).  This does not invoke DPTLK.  Given lookup value, this returns a list of the form DFN^patient name^DOB^PID.  Only the first 500 records that match the value are returned.

rpcs.name: SC POSITION MEMBERS
rpcs.description: Returns an array of positions.  For each position, the standard role, |TAB|  7:  Primary Care role (if any) for position. team member currently filling, and the preceptor (if any) for the team member.   Returns both Internal and external values. Piece 1:  Team Ien |TAB|  2:  Position Ien |TAB|  3:  Team member Ien (File #200)', "|TAB|  4:  Team member's name", "      5:  Precpetor's Ien (File #200)", "|TAB|  6:  Preceptor's Name

rpcs.name: SC FILE SINGLE VALUE
rpcs.description: File a single value, similar to DIE stuff.  For those instances where a single value needs to be filed or changed, and that value is not associated with a specific component on the client side. ie changing a flag.
rpcs.returnDescription: 1 if Update ok, 0 if the update was not completed.

rpcs.name: SCTM AUTOLINK GETRECORD
rpcs.description: This API returns the 0th node of the Team file(#404.51) entry and the list of current autolinks.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array contains the 0th node for the Team file(#404.51) 1- AutoLink Display Text 2- Autolink Type 3- AutoLink Entity IEN  Normally, the display text value is the .01 field but when more identifying information is needed, then this string could contain more. entry and the list of entities autolinked t

rpcs.name: SC KEY CHECK
rpcs.description: Return 1 if the user is assigned the key passed in, else return 0

rpcs.name: SCTM AUTOLINK SETRECORD
rpcs.description: This API sets the list of autolinks for a team.
rpcs.returnDescription: Indicates that the autolink set process was successful(1) or unsuccessful(0).

rpcs.name: SCTM AUTOLINK GETLINK
rpcs.description: This rpc returns the list of Team names that are autolinked to an entity.
rpcs.returnDescription: List of Teams for which  the entity is autolinked.  The value of each item in the array is the Team name.

rpcs.name: SCRP DEFINITION GETRECORD
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a scheduling report definition as defined in file 404.92.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned: [Sorts

rpcs.name: SCUT GET USER RECORD
rpcs.description: This RPC returns user related data often needed by scheduling modules. The data returned is used to set instance variables for the TScUser object type on the client.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned: SCDATA(0) -> <ien of user> ^ <name of user> ^ <default query id>  --- OR ---  SCDATA(0) -> 0 - meaning errors found ^ <number of errors> SCDATA(1...n) -> error text

rpcs.name: SCRP QUERY GETRECORD
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a scheduling report query template definition as definedin file 404.95.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned:  [Fields

rpcs.name: SCRP SELECTION SOURCE
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list of entries that is displayed to the user as part of the entry selection process. This process occurs during query template setup. It involves the user indicating which divsions, teams, roles, clinics, practitioners and user classes s/he desires information about on a report.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned: SCDATA(1...n) -> <name of entry> ^ <type of entity> ^ <internal entry number of entity>

rpcs.name: SCRP FILE ENTRY GETSELECTION
rpcs.description: This RPC returns information assoicated with an entry on the entity selection form of the Query Template Utility.  The RPC gets invoked as a result of the user clicking the', "right mouse button and selecting 'Details'.
rpcs.returnDescription: This RPC passes back an array of information for the entity. The information is enity type specific. For example, if the selection is a DIVISION entity then a least of Teams for that Division are returned.   Using SCDATA to represent the array, the data is returned in nodes SCDATA(1..n).

rpcs.name: SCRP QUERY SAVE
rpcs.description: This RPC attempts to save the query template definition for the user. Before saving query, the RPC will validate the query criteria and report any errors without saving query.  The query template definition must pass the validation check before it can be saved.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the return array, the following is the definition of the data being passed back via this RPC:  SCDATA(0) -> 1 - meaning deletion was successful ^ <new query ien> ^ <1/0 reload client flag(some inappropriate data was stripped during save>  --- OR --- -> 0 - meaning errors fo

rpcs.name: SCRP QUERY VALIDATE
rpcs.description: This RPC will validate the criteria specified in the query template definiton passed in as part of the call. The validation checks the criteria against what data is required the report assoicated with the template.  If the validation check fails, then the errors found are returned as part of the RPC, otherwise a success flag(1) is returned.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned: SCDATA(0) -> 1 - meaning validation checks found no errors  --- OR ---  SCDATA(0) -> 0 - meaning errors found ^ <number of errors> SCDATA(1...n) -> error text

rpcs.name: SCRP QUERY DELETE
rpcs.description: This RPC allows for the deletion of a query, usually at the request of the user.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the return array, the following is the definition of the data being passed back via this RPC:  SCDATA(0) -> 1 - meaning deletion was successful --- OR --- -> 0 - meaning errors found ^ <number of errors>  SCDATA(1...n) -> error text

rpcs.name: SCRP QUERY CHECK NAME
rpcs.description: This RPC checks to see if the user already has a query with the name being passed in as part of the RPC.  If there is already a query with the same name, the RPC passes back the internal entry number of the RPC, otherwise the RPC passes back a 0.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the return array, the following is found for the name passed in for the user. the definition of the data being passed back via this RPC:  SCDATA(1) -> 0 means no query with the passed in name was found for the user  --- OR ---  <n> where n is the internal entry number of th

rpcs.name: SCUT SET USER QUERY DEFAULT
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to set or change the user's defualt PCMM", 'query template.
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned: SCDATA(0) -> 1 - meaning that deafult was successfully set/changed  --- OR ---  SCDATA(0) -> 0 - meaning errors found ^ <number of errors> SCDATA(1...n) -> error text

rpcs.name: SCRP REPORT PRINT
rpcs.description: This RPC queues a report to print based the is appropriate for the report. If the validation fails, the errors are returned as part of the RPC. the query template definition passed in as part ithe RPC call.  The RPC returns the TaskMan task number.  Before the report assoicated with the query template is queued to print, it must pass a validation check. This check makes sure the criteria in the template
rpcs.returnDescription: Using SCDATA to represent the array, the following is returned: SCDATA(0) -> TaskMan task number assicated with queued report  --- OR if errors were found during validation ---  SCDATA(0) -> 0 - meaning errors found ^ <number of errors> SCDATA(1...n) -> error text

rpcs.name: SC STAFF LOOKUP
rpcs.description: Returns a list of staff members.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns IEN^NAME^TITLE^USER CLASS

rpcs.name: SC USER CLASS STATUS
rpcs.description: Returns a 1 if the User class is installed and in use, a 0 if the User Class is not installed

rpcs.name: SC PRIMARY CARE TEAM
rpcs.description: Returns the primary care team for a patient for the date specified. Pass in the DFN and Date.  Returns team information.

rpcs.name: SC GET PAT BLOCK
rpcs.description: Retrieve a block of patients available to be assigned from the ^TMP holding global built by the SC BLD PAT.... remote procedure call. Input $J, the starting number in the block, the ending number in the block, and the last entry in the global.

rpcs.name: SC BLD PAT LIST
rpcs.description: Builds a list of patients to be assigned to either a team or position through the multiple assignment form.  This list is called by the actual "filer" for either the Team or the position.  The list\'s format is ^TMP($J,"SC PATIENT LIST",DFN)

rpcs.name: SC FILE PAT TM ASGN
rpcs.description: File patient team assignments in ^TMP("SC PATIENT LIST",DFN) global for later processing

rpcs.name: SC BLD PAT CLN LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC build a list of patients by assignment to a selected clinic. Lookup is in the Hospital location file, screening of "C" in the (0,3) node.  List is stored in ^TMP($J,"SCCLPT",N)=DFN.  List is alphabetized by name.

rpcs.name: SC FILE PAT POS ASGN
rpcs.description: File patient position assignments in ^TMP("SC PATIENT LIST",DFN) global for later processing

rpcs.name: SC BLD PAT SCDE LIST
rpcs.description: Buils a list of patients who are associated with a particular stop code. Only those patients within the specified date range will appear in the list. Screens out inactive stop codes.  Format of the list is ^TMP($J,"SCSCDE",DFN)

rpcs.name: SC BLD PAT TM LIST
rpcs.description: Builds a list of patients who are assigned to the selected team.  format of the list is ^TMP($J,"SCTEAM",n)=DFN.

rpcs.name: SC BLD PAT POS LIST
rpcs.description: Build a list of patients who are assigned to a selected position. The format of the list is ^TMP($J,"SCPOS",n)=DFN.  This list is called the actual filer through another RPC.

rpcs.name: SC PAT ENROLL CLN
rpcs.description: Enrolls patient in the clinic that is associated with the position the patient is being assigned to.  Pass in DFN, Clinic, required fields, and the date assigned.

rpcs.name: SC FILE ALL PAT TM ASGN
rpcs.description: File patient - team assignments in ^TMP("SC PATIENT LIST",DFN) global

rpcs.name: SC BLD PAT APT LIST
rpcs.description: Builds a list of patients for a selected clinic by appointment date range. This list is stored in ^TMP($J,"SCAPP",n)=DFN.  List is in alphabetical order.

rpcs.name: SC FILE ALL PAT POS ASGN
SC BLD NOPC TM LIST
rpcs.description: Builds a list of patients that have a primary care assignment, but no current primary care team assignment.  Uses $$PTPCNTM^SCAPMC20

rpcs.name: SC PAT ASGN MAILMAN
rpcs.description: Builds the before and after status for a single patient assignment to either a team or a position, and fires off a mailman message.

rpcs.name: IBD EXPAND FORMID
rpcs.description: This call will convert a form id (printed at the top of an encounter form) and return information about the patient, the appointment, and the form. See routine IBDRPC3 for output descritpion.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a 17 piece record, see IBDRPC3 for details.

rpcs.name: IBD GET FORMSPEC
rpcs.description: This call will return the Form Specification file (as an array) that is used by the scanning software to recognize a form.  Lines longer than 255 characters are truncated on the server and flagged with "~~~ indicating the need to concatenate on the client.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array that is to be saved on the client as a formspec file.

rpcs.name: IBD GET INPUT OBJECT BY CLINIC
rpcs.description: Returns a list of input objects on all encounter forms defined for a clinic in the clinic setup.  There are 3 types of input objects, lists, hand print fields, and multiple choice fields.  Each has its own set of unique characterists for input.
rpcs.returnDescription: Output is an array of input objects from the forms, each node is an 8 piece record.  See IBDFRPC for details.

rpcs.name: IBD GET INPUT OBJECT BY FORM
rpcs.description: Returns a list of input objects on one encounter forms . There are 3 types of input objects, lists, hand print fields, and multiple choice fields.  Each has its own set of unique characterists for input.
rpcs.returnDescription: Output is an array of input objects from the form, each node is an 8 piece record.  See IBDFRPC for details.

rpcs.name: IBD GET ONE INPUT OBJECT
rpcs.description: This call will return specific information about any one input object 1 := exactly one 2 := at most one (0 or 1) 3 := at least one (1 or more)  There are also a number of data qualifiers that are used to describe the data, for example, primary, secondary, add to problem list, etc. regardless of type.  Developers should first use the RPC to return the available input objects (either by form or by clinic).  There are 3 types of input objects, lists (both static and dynamic), hand print fields, and
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns array describing input object.  See routine IBDFRPC1 for details. See routine IBDFDE* for examples of use.  (ibdfde2* for lists, ibdfde3 for hand print fields, ibdfde4 for multiple choice fields)  The return array is different for the different types of objects.

rpcs.name: IBD RECEIVE FORM DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC is to be used by the AICS workstation software only.  It accepts data in the format returned by the scanning software and converts the data in the format expected by PCE and passes the data to PCE.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array of data describing the input data in human readable terms and any errors and warnings sent by PCE.

rpcs.name: IBD VALIDATE USER
rpcs.description: This call is used to validate user information and for display on the Show User screen.
rpcs.returnDescription: returns an 11 piece record of user/system information

rpcs.name: IBD RECEIVE DATA
rpcs.description: This call is the public RPC for developers to input data from an encounter form to AICS for processing to PCE.  See routine IBDFRPC4 for detailed description of variables.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns status of passing of data to PCE.

rpcs.name: IBD RETURN IMAGE ID
rpcs.description: Returns a numeric between 1 and 9999999 that is used as part of the file name to store the next unknown image as.
rpcs.returnDescription: numeric

rpcs.name: IBD ELAPSED TIME
rpcs.description: Store elapsed time for data entry

rpcs.name: IBD STORE WORKSTATION ERROR
rpcs.description: This call back is used by AICS to log entries in the AICS ERROR AND WARNING FILE when the recognition of a form is canceled or otherwise interrupted.

rpcs.name: IBD STORE IMAGE NAME
rpcs.description: This RPC will cause the information on images saved to be stored in the AICS IMAGE LOG file.

rpcs.name: TIU NOTES
rpcs.description: This API gets lists of progress notes for a patient, with optional parameters for STATUS, EARLY DATE/TIME, and LATE DATE/TIME.

rpcs.name: TIU GET RECORD TEXT
rpcs.description: This RPC will get the textual portion of a TIU Document Record.

rpcs.name: TIU SUMMARIES
rpcs.description: This API gets lists of Discharge Summaries for a patient, with optional parameters for STATUS, EARLY DATE/TIME, and LATE DATE/TIME.

rpcs.name: TIU NOTES BY VISIT
rpcs.description: This API gets lists of Progress Notes by visit from TIU.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is the pointer to the visit file.

rpcs.name: TIU SUMMARIES BY VISIT
rpcs.description: This API returns lists of Discharge Summaries by visit.

rpcs.name: TIU GET PN TITLES
rpcs.description: This API returns a list of Progress Notes Titles, including a SHORT LIST of preferred titles as defined by the user, and a LONG LIST of all titles defined at the site.

rpcs.name: TIU LOAD BOILERPLATE TEXT
rpcs.description: This RPC will load the boilerplate text associated with the selected title, and execute the methods for any objects embedded in the boilerplate text.

rpcs.name: TIU SIGN RECORD
rpcs.description: This API Supports the application of the user's electronic signature to a", "TIU document while evaluating authorization, and validating the user's", 'electronic signature.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is the error code which may result if the user enters an invalid code, or if the Authorization/Subscription Utility determines that the user is NOT authorized to sign (or cosign) the document, as specified by', "the site's business rules.

rpcs.name: TIU GET PERSONAL PREFERENCES
rpcs.description: Returns Users personal preferences for TIU in the following format:  TIUY = USER [1P
rpcs.returnDescription: TIUY = USER [1P

rpcs.name: TIU UPDATE RECORD
rpcs.description: This API updates the record named in the TIUDA parameter, with the information contained in the TIUX(Field #) array.  The body of the modified TIU document should be passed in the TIUX("TEXT",i,0) subscript, where i is the line number (i.e., the "TEXT" node should be ready to MERGE with a word processing field).  Any filing errors which may occur will be returned in the single valued ERR parameter (which is passed by reference).

rpcs.name: TIU REQUIRES COSIGNATURE
rpcs.description: This Boolean RPC simply evaluates whether the current user requires cosignature for TIU DOCUMENTS, and returns a 1 if true, or a 0 if false.
rpcs.returnDescription: Boolean result: 0 if FALSE, OR 1 if TRUE.

rpcs.name: TIU LOAD RECORD FOR EDIT
rpcs.description: This RPC loads the return array with data in a format consistent with that required by the TIU UPDATE RECORD API.  It should be invoked when the user invokes the Edit action, to load the dialog for editing the document.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return array will be subscripted by the field #, with the body of the document in the ^TMP("TIUEDIT",$J,"TEXT",n) subscript.

rpcs.name: TIU DETAILED DISPLAY
rpcs.description: Gets details for display of a given record.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns all known details of the record identified by TIUDA (Source, Signature, Linked Problems, Body of document, etc.).

rpcs.name: TIU CREATE ADDENDUM RECORD
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure allows the creation of addenda to TIU Documents.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is the record number of the resulting addendum.  NOTE:  If no addendum record may be created, then the return variable will look as follows:  "-1^Could not create addendum."

rpcs.name: TIU CREATE RECORD
rpcs.description: This remote procedure allows the creation of TIU DOCUMENT records.
rpcs.returnDescription: If the call is successful, this will be the record number (IEN) of the resulting entry in the TIU DOCUMENT FILE (#8925).  In the event of a filing error, the first "^"-piece will be zero, and the second "^"-piece of this scalar return variable will be a textual message describing the nature of the e

rpcs.name: TIU DELETE RECORD
rpcs.description: Deletes TIU Document records...Evaluates authorization.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns error message with ERR=1^Explanation text if the user is NOT', "authorized to delete the named record (e.g., it's his, but signed; or it's", 'not his, and he better keep his paws off it).

rpcs.name: TIU PRINT RECORD
rpcs.description: Allows Printing of TIU Documents on demand.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is an error flag/message which will have two '^'-pieces.  The first", 'piece will be a boolean flag indicating whether or not the call failed (0 indicates success, while 1 indicates failure).  The second piece is a textual message indicating the nature of the error.  In the event of success, th

rpcs.name: TIU GET DOCUMENT PARAMETERS
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure returns the parameters by which a given document or document type is to be processed.
rpcs.returnDescription: This call returns an array with just one element (so far).  The string ==>[9S

rpcs.name: TIU GET DS URGENCIES
rpcs.description: Returns a set of discharge summary urgencies for use in a long list box.
rpcs.returnDescription: The array of urgencies are returned in the following format:  Y(1) = <internal value> ^ <external value>   Example: Y(1) = P^priority Y(2) = R^routine

rpcs.name: TIU AUTHORIZATION
rpcs.description: This RPC allows the calling application to evaluate privilege to perform any ASU-mediated action on a TIU document.
rpcs.returnDescription: Boolean Truth value, with explanatory message in second '^'-piece if", 'FALSE.  That is:  When privilege to perform action on document is granted, TIUY=1  When privilege to perform action on document is denied, TIUY=0^Why not

rpcs.name: TIU DOCUMENTS BY CONTEXT
rpcs.description: Returns lists of TIU Documents that satisfy the following search criteria:  1 - signed documents (all) 2 - unsigned documents 3 - uncosigned documents 4 - signed documents/author 5 - signed documents/date range
rpcs.returnDescription: The return array has elements with the following positional values: date range)), then a final data element will be added indicating more are available with the following positional values:  IEN^"SHOW MORE"^^^^^^^^^^^^^  IEN^TITLE^REFERENCE DATE/TIME (INT)^PATIENT NAME (LAST I/LAST 4)^AUTHOR (INT;EX

rpcs.name: TIU NOTES 16 BIT
rpcs.description: This API gets lists of progress notes for a patient, with optional parameters for STATUS, EARLY DATE/TIME, and LATE DATE/TIME.

rpcs.name: TIU WHICH SIGNATURE ACTION
rpcs.description: This RPC infers whether the user is trying to sign or cosign the docuement in question, and indicates which ASU ACTION the GUI should pass to the TIU AUTHORIZATION RPC.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a single valued string, indicating whether the client application should pass SIGNATURE or COSIGNATURE to the TIU AUTHORIZATION RPC.

rpcs.name: XWB EXAMPLE ECHO STRING
rpcs.description: This RPC receives a string which will be sent right back to the client. It exists for support of RPC Broker Example program.

rpcs.name: XWB EXAMPLE GET LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC brings back a list of elements to the client.  The user can request either a number of lines or a number of Kilobytes of data to be returned. This RPC exists support of RPC Broker Example program.

rpcs.name: XUS KEY CHECK
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6286 - API ICR #3277 This RPC will check if the user (DUZ) holds a security key or an array of keys. If a single security KEY is sent the result is returned in R(0). If an array is sent down then the return array has the same order as the calling array.

rpcs.name: XUS CVC
rpcs.description: RPC ICR #6296 - API ICR #none This RPC is used as part of Kernel to allow the user to change their verify code.
rpcs.returnDescription: R(0) = Zero if VC was changed, 1 if it could not be changed. R(1) = Error message if VC could not be changed.

rpcs.name: XUS AV HELP
rpcs.description: Returns instructions on entering new access/verify codes.

rpcs.name: IBD GET ALL PCE DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to retrieve all data in PCE for a particlar encounter formatted for display in a memo component.

rpcs.name: IBD GET PAST APPT LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC can be used to return a list of appointments for a patient.  It', "defaults to returning the past 1 year's kept appointments.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the list of appointments in reverse chronological order, p1 := appointment date (external) p2 := clinic (external) p3 := status (external) p4 := appt type (external) p5 := appointment date (internal) p6 := clinic (internal) p7 := status (internal) p8 := appt type (internal)

rpcs.name: SDOE GET DIAGNOSES
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure Call (RPC) returns an array of diagnoses', "This RPC will attempt to find this 'old' data, reformat the data", 'to meet the V POV structure and return the list of diagnoses as described above.  (Only the diagnosis code internal entry number', "is available for 'old' encounters.)", 'for an encounter.  Note: For encounters before 10/1/96, only scheduling data in the OUTPATIENT DIAGNOSIS (#409.43) file may exist. It will only exist if the site required diagnoses as part of the
rpcs.returnDescription: The 0th node of a record in the V POV file.  Example Array Item: 97^101^459^192^^^^^^^^P

rpcs.name: SDOE GET PROVIDERS
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure Call (RPC) returns a subscripted array of providers data to meet the V PROVIDER structure and return the list of providers as described above. (Only the provider internal entry', "number is available for 'old' encounters.)", 'for an encounter. rpcs.name: TIU IS THIS A CONSULT?
rpcs.description: BOOLEAN RPC which evaluates whether the title indicated is that of a consult.
rpcs.returnDescription: Scalar value indicating the truth value of the result (i.e., 0 indicates FALSE - the title is NOT that of a CONSULT; 1 indicates TRUE - the title IS that of a consult).

rpcs.name: TIU LONG LIST OF TITLES
rpcs.description: This RPC serves data to a longlist of selectable TITLES by CLASS.  e.g., passing the class PROGRESS NOTES will return active Progress Notes titles which the current user is authorized to enter notes under.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is an array of the 44 nearest titles to that indicate by the user in the direction passed by the longlist component.

rpcs.name: TIU PERSONAL TITLE LIST
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure returns the user's list of preferred titles for a", 'given class of documents, along with the default title, if specified.

rpcs.name: TIU LONG LIST CONSULT TITLES
rpcs.description: This RPC serves data to a longlist of selectable TITLES for CONSULTS.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is an array of the 44 nearest titles to that indicate by the user in the direction passed by the longlist component.

rpcs.name: TIU IDENTIFY CONSULTS CLASS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the record number of the class CONSULTS in the TIU DOCUMENT DEFINITION file (#8925.1).
rpcs.returnDescription: The value returned will be either a positive integer, corresponding to the record number identifying the CONSULTS class, or zero, indicating that CONSULTS are not yet implemented in TIU.

rpcs.name: DDR GETS ENTRY DATA
rpcs.description: Calls database server at GETS^DIQ.

rpcs.name: DDR FILER
rpcs.description: Generic call to file edits into FM file.
rpcs.returnDescription: If file update is successful, then a 1 is returned. A 0 is returned if unsuccessful.

rpcs.name: DDR VALIDATOR
rpcs.description: This function allows the application to validate user input to a field before filing data. The call uses the database server VAL^DIE call.
rpcs.returnDescription: This call passes back information in the [data

rpcs.name: DDR DELETE ENTRY
rpcs.description: This function deletes an entry in a FileMan file using ^DIK.
rpcs.returnDescription: This parameter returns 1 if record was deleted else it returns 0.

rpcs.name: DDR LOCK/UNLOCK NODE
rpcs.description: This function will lock or unlock an M global node.  Also, this function allows the calling application to specify the', "timeout (in seconds) for a 'lock' command.
rpcs.returnDescription: This parameter returns 1 if the lock or unlock command is successful, otherwise a 0 is returned.

rpcs.name: DDR FIND1
rpcs.description: This function returns the internal entry number of a record using $$FIND1^DIC.
rpcs.returnDescription: This parameter returns a valid internal record number if a match is found, a 0 if no match was found or a -1 if an error occurred. ', "(For now, this is 'single value'.  It will return the error array", 'later.)

rpcs.name: SC ACTIVE POSITION ASSIGNMENTS
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to obtain the active positions associated with a patient team assignment.
rpcs.returnDescription: The results parameter will contain a global reference that will contain the Refer to the routine for details  following information: IEN of Team Position file Name of position Current effective date Pointer to role Name of role Pointer to user class Name of user class

rpcs.name: TIU GET ADDITIONAL SIGNERS
rpcs.description: Returns the list of additional signers currently identified for a given TIU document.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is an array of persons whose signature is expected on the document. e.g.,  TIUY(1)=213^CASEY,BEN TIUY(2)=73425^HOWSER,DOOGEY TIUY(3)=172^WELBY,MARCUS  Note that the array elements are returned as DUZ^Username.

rpcs.name: TIU UPDATE ADDITIONAL SIGNERS
rpcs.description: This RPC accepts a list of persons, and adds them as additional signers for the document identified by the first parameter.
rpcs.returnDescription: '^'-delimited String, which includes the DUZ's of the users who were", 'successfully added as additional signers.

rpcs.name: TIU LOCK RECORD
rpcs.description: This RPC will issue an incremental LOCK on the record identified by the TIUDA parameter, returning an integer truth value indicating success or failure in obtaining the LOCK.
rpcs.returnDescription: ERR flag indicating whether or not an incremental LOCK was obtained:  0 if the LOCK was GRANTED 1^<Explanatory Message> if LOCK was DENIED

rpcs.name: TIU UNLOCK RECORD
rpcs.description: This RPC will decrement the lock on a given TIU Document Record, identified by the TIUDA input parameter. The return value will always be 0.
rpcs.returnDescription: Since decrementing a lock never fails, return value will always be 0.

rpcs.name: TIU CAN CHANGE COSIGNER?
rpcs.description: BOOLEAN RPC to evaluate user's privilege to modify the expected cosigner, ", "given the current status of the document, and the user's role with respect ", 'to it.
rpcs.returnDescription: Boolean Truth value, with explanatory message in second '^'-piece if", ' FALSE.  That is:  When privilege to change the Expected Cosigner is granted, TIUY=1 When privilege to change the Expected Cosigner is denied, TIUY=0^Why not

rpcs.name: TIU JUSTIFY DELETE?
rpcs.description: BOOLEAN RPC that evaluates wheter a justification is required for deletion (e.g., deletion is authorized, but the document has been signed, etc.).
rpcs.returnDescription: Single valued, BOOLEAN return variable, where 1 indicates that the calling application should ask for a justification, and 0 indicates that no justification is needed.

rpcs.name: TIU GET ALERT INFO
rpcs.description: Given a TIU XQAID, return the patient and document type for the item being alerted.
rpcs.returnDescription: TIUDA^DFN^gui tab indicator  where  TIUDA is the document IEN in ^TIU(8925 DFN is the patient IEN gui tab indicator is an arbitrarily set constant based on the document type.

rpcs.name: XWB IS RPC AVAILABLE
rpcs.description: If RPC is installed, available in relevant context, and of proper version returns 1. Otherwise, returns 0.  Integration Agreement (IA) #3011
rpcs.returnDescription: Boolean.  1 = RPC available. 0 = RPC not available.

rpcs.name: XWB ARE RPCS AVAILABLE
rpcs.description: If RPC in the passed array is installed, available in relevant context, and of proper version returns 1 for that RPC. Otherwise, returns 0.  Integration Agreement (IA) #3012
rpcs.returnDescription: A 0-based array.  The index corresponds to the index of the RPC in the RPC Input Parameter.  A value of 1 means the corresponding RPC is available; a value of 0 means it is not available.

rpcs.name: XWB REMOTE RPC
rpcs.description: This is the RPC that is called to request that an application RPC be run on a remote system.  The data is passed by HL7 to the remote system as is the return value.  This RPC will return a HANDLE that can be used to check if the data has been sent back from the remote system.  The HANDLE can be used in another RPC to check the status of the RPC.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is always an array.  The first node of the array is equal to a string that serves as a HANDLE. This is used to check the status of a RPC request and to retrieve the results of the RPC. In the case of an error condition, the first node of the array is equal to a string with the synta

rpcs.name: XWB REMOTE STATUS CHECK
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the status of a remote RPC.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is always an array.  The first node of the array is The second node of the array is the status from the HL7 package. equal to one of the following values: "-1^Bad Handle" - An invalid handle has been passed. "0^New" - The request has been sent. "0^Running" - HL7 indicates that the m

rpcs.name: XWB REMOTE GETDATA
rpcs.description: This RPC will return an ARRAY with what ever data has been sent back from the remote site.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is the array of data.  In the event of an error condition, the first node of the array is equal to a string with the syntax "-1^error text".

rpcs.name: XWB DEFERRED RPC
rpcs.description: This is the RPC that is called to request that a RPC be run through taskman in the background.  The first parameter is the name of the RPC to be run.  The next 1-10 parameters are the ones for the RPC.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is always an array.  The first node of the array is equal to a string that serves as a HANDLE. This is used to check the status of a RPC request and to retrieve the results of the RPC. In the case of an error condition, the first node of the array is equal to a string with the synta

rpcs.name: XWB DEFERRED STATUS
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the status of a deferred RPC.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is always an array.  The first node of the array is equal to one of the following values: "-1^Bad Handle" - An invalid handle has been passed. "O^New" - The request has been sent. "0^Running" - RPC is still processing "1^Done" - RPC has completed and the data has returned to the loc

rpcs.name: XWB DEFERRED GETDATA
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to return the data from the XWB DEFERRED RPC call.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is the array of data.  In the event of an error condition, the first node of the array is equal to a string with the syntax "-1^error text".

rpcs.name: XWB DIRECT RPC
rpcs.description: This is the Broker RPC that is called to request that a RPC be run on a remote system.  The data is passed by HL7 to the remote system as is the return value.  The difference between this and the XWB REMOTE RPC is this', "is a blocking call meaning the user's workstation will not process", 'anything else until the data returns from the remote system.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is the array of data. In the case of an error condition, the first node of the array is equal to a string with the syntax "-1^error text".

rpcs.name: XWB REMOTE CLEAR

rpcs.name: XWB DEFERRED CLEAR
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to CLEAR the data under a handle in the ^XTMP global.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is always an array.  The first node of the array is equal to 1.

rpcs.name: XWB DEFERRED CLEARALL
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to CLEAR all the data known to this job in the ^XTMP global.  Makes use of the list in ^TMP("XWBHDL",$J,handle).
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is always an array.  The first node of the array is equal to 1.

rpcs.name: TIU ID CAN ATTACH
rpcs.description: This BOOLEAN RPC evaluates the question of whether a particular document may be attached as an entry to an Interdisciplinary Note (i.e., can this document be an ID Child?).
rpcs.returnDescription: This is the BOOLEAN result:  1                      = Document may be attached 0^Explanatory message  = Document may NOT be attached -1^Explanatory message = Document may NOT be attached, because a business rule allows it to also be a parent document.

rpcs.name: TIU ID CAN RECEIVE
rpcs.description: This BOOLEAN RPC evaluates the question of whether a particular document may receive an entry as an Interdisciplinary Parent Note (i.e., can this document be an ID Parent?).
rpcs.returnDescription: This is the BOOLEAN result:  1                     = Document may receive an ID Entry 0^Explanatory message = Document may NOT receive an ID Entry

rpcs.name: TIU ID ATTACH ENTRY
rpcs.description: This RPC will attach a a document as an Interdisciplinary (ID) entry to an ID Parent document.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is a flag indicating the success or failure of the call:  IF THE CALL SUCCEEDS, THE ENTRY NUMBER OF THE ENTRY WILL BE RETURNED.  IF THE CALL FAILS, 0^Explanatory Message WILL BE RETURNED.

rpcs.name: TIU ID DETACH ENTRY
rpcs.description: This call will remove an ID Entry from an Interdisciplinary Note.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is a flag indicating the success or failure of the call:  IF THE CALL SUCCEEDS, THE ENTRY NUMBER OF THE ENTRY WILL BE RETURNED. IF THE CALL FAILS, 0^Explanatory Message WILL BE RETURNED.

rpcs.returnDescription: Array in the format:  1: Node 0 of TIU Template Field File (#8927.1) 2: D^Description Records 3: I^Item Records

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD LIST
rpcs.description: Returns long list array of template fields

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD CAN EDIT
rpcs.description: Returns TRUE if the current user is allowed to edit template fields.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD LOCK
rpcs.description: Locks a template field record for editing
rpcs.returnDescription: Flag returns success of lock.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD UNLOCK
rpcs.description: Unlock Template Field

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD SAVE
rpcs.description: Saves a single Template Field
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns success or failuer of save operation.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD IMPORT
rpcs.description: Imports Template Fields from XML format
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list of the template fields imported, and the success of each import.  Some template fields may be renamed in the process.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD EXPORT
rpcs.description: Exports Template Fields in XML format
rpcs.returnDescription: XML version of template fields.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD LOAD BY IEN
rpcs.description: Returns a single Template Field object.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array in the format:  1: Node 0 of TIU Template Field File (#8927.1) 2: D^Description Records 3: I^Item Records

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD NAME IS UNIQUE
rpcs.description: Returns TRUE if the template field name is unique

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD DELETE
rpcs.description: Deletes an entry in the Template Field (8927.1) file.

rpcs.name: TIU REMINDER DIALOGS
rpcs.description: Returns a list of reminder dialogs allowed for use as Templates.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array of allowed reminder dialogs in IEN^Dialog Name format.

rpcs.name: TIU REM DLG OK AS TEMPLATE
rpcs.description: Returns TRUE is the passed in reminder dialog is allowed to be used in a TIU Template.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD DOLMTEXT
rpcs.description: Reads through an array of text and converts all entries of template fields to their assocaited List Manager text values.
rpcs.returnDescription: Same input array with template fields removed.

rpcs.name: TIU TEMPLATE PERSONAL OBJECTS
rpcs.description: Returns a list or Patient Data Objects allowed in Personal Templates.

rpcs.name: TIU TEMPLATE LOCK
rpcs.description: Locks Template
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns success of lock.

rpcs.name: TIU TEMPLATE UNLOCK
rpcs.description: Unlocks a template.

rpcs.name: TIU USER CLASS LONG LIST
rpcs.description: Long List of User Classes

rpcs.name: TIU DIV AND CLASS INFO
rpcs.description: Returns a list of Divisions and User Classes for a specific User.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array in the following format: D^Division IEN C^User Class IEN First Division should be DUZ(2).

rpcs.name: IBARXM QUERY ONLY
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to query only the information for pharmacy co-payment billing that has happened for the given month/year.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return array is in file 354.71 format.

rpcs.name: IBARXM TRANS DATA
rpcs.description: This remote procedure is used to receive transaction information from a remote facility and reply with an acknowledgement. This RPC call does not send user data to remote side.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return is the transaction IEN on this facility, indicating acceptance of the transmission.  Otherwise a 0 is returned.

rpcs.name: PXRM REMINDER DIALOG (TIU)
rpcs.description: Dialog for a given dialog ien.
rpcs.returnDescription: For each dialog element/group in the reminder dialog:  1) Initial states  1^dialog item ien[#801.41

rpcs.description: List reminders and categories in display order for a reminder category.

rpcs.name: PXRM REMINDERS AND CATEGORIES
rpcs.description: Returns list of reminders and categories.

rpcs.name: ORQPT DEFAULT CLINIC DATE RANG
rpcs.description: Returns default start and stop dates for clinics in the form: start^stop. Start and stop are free text and are not in FM format.
rpcs.returnDescription: Default start and stop dates for clinics in the form: start^stop.  Start and stop are free text and are not in FM format.  For example, "T-1" may be used.

rpcs.name: ORQQCN REMOVABLE MED RESULTS
rpcs.description: Returns list of medicine results that are currently attached to a procedure.
rpcs.returnDescription: List of medicine results in the format:  19;MCAR(691.5,^ELECTROCARDIOGRAM^JUN 30,1999^ABNORMAL  where piece 1 = variable pointer to result 2 = name of result 3 = date of result 4 = interpretation of result

rpcs.name: ORQQCN GET MED RESULT DETAILS

rpcs.name: ORQQCN ATTACH MED RESULTS
rpcs.description: Allows a med result to be attached to a procedure request.

rpcs.name: ORQQCN REMOVE MED RESULTS
rpcs.description: Allows removal of medicine results from a  procedure.

rpcs.name: ORWORB KILL EXPIR OI ALERT
rpcs.description: Evaluate expiring flagged orderable item orders. If none remain, kill current alert for current user.  Kill for other users if alert so defined.

rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array formatted as follows-  svc ien^svc name or syn^parent^has children^svc usage^orderable item

rpcs.description: Returns "1" if TIU*1.0*100 is installed, "0" otherwise.  This lets the CPRS GUI enable or disable the Interdisciplinary Notes functionality until the server is ready.
rpcs.returnDescription: See description.

rpcs.name: ORQQPX GET FOLDERS

rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the value of the ORQQPX REMINDER FOLDERS parameter

rpcs.returnDescription: 1 if Encounters can be entered at any time.

rpcs.returnDescription: 1^      - document can be an ID child 0^error - document cannot be an ID child, and reason.

If the GUI is the PREVIOUS version and falls outside of the grace period or the GUI is not the MINIMUM or PREVIOUS build this RPC will return the grace period date of January 1, 1980 enforcing GUI version control to recognize this version is no longer allowed sign on.

rpcs.description: This will mark records in file 228.1 as deleted.   Through indexes on the DATE DELETED field in file 228.1, the associated transactions in file 228.2 will be marked as deleted.  Since these two files are considered part of the official medical record, then, according to HIPAA, once a permenant record has been filed, it cannot be deleted unless an audit trail is maintained.  So we will keep these deleted records in these two files but will mark the records as having been deleted.

rpcs.name: DENTV FILE DATA

rpcs.returnDescription: The return array data will be in the format n^text where n = -1 if an error or other unacceptable condition encountered for DAS & dental history n = ien to corresponding file for pce, return 1^PCE<message>^visit ien

rpcs.name: DENTV GET CATEG/CODES
rpcs.description: This works in conjunction with the DENTV GET CATEGORIES rpc.  You pass the subcategory name and this call will return the array of cpt codes associated with that subcategory name.  end description update 6-23-2003
rpcs.returnDescription: An array of cpt codes is returned that have this subcategory (fields 5 or 6 in file 228).  The global array returns data in ^TMP("DENTV",$J).  For a descripiton of the data return format see GETADA^DENTVRP1.

rpcs.name: DENTV GET CATEGORIES
rpcs.description: This rpc will return the different categories (fields 5 & 6) in file 228
rpcs.returnDescription: An array is returned (in alphabetical order) of the various subcategory values found in file 228 (fields 5 and 6) array(#) = subcategory name

rpcs.name: DENTV GET CODE LIST
rpcs.description: This call will return all ADA CPT codes which match the inputted string.', "You can pass the cpt code name or a string, like 'surf'.
rpcs.returnDescription: An array of codes will be returned.  For format of that array see the return description in GETADA^DENTVRP1.  Two possible error messages may be returned:', " 1. If the lookup value you send is null, then '-1^No input string sent'", '    will be returned. 2. If a lookup value was sent, but no matche

rpcs.name: DENTV TOOTH HISTORY
rpcs.description: This returns the dental history for a single tooth for a patient or for all records.
rpcs.returnDescription: A global array will be passed back to the calling client.  The format order (newest record first). p3 :== cpt short description p4 :== cpt code', "  p5 :== provider's name", '  p6 :== surfaces p7 :== quadrant p8 :== # canals', "  p9 :== primary (this will be null or equal to 'YES')", ' p10 :== pce p

rpcs.name: DENTV DENTAL PROVIDER
rpcs.description: From user's duz, verify that the user is a valid dental provider
rpcs.returnDescription: This will return the user's name if the user is an active dental", 'provider.  If the user is not, then it returns -1 This will return the SIGNATURE BLOCK PRINTED NAME if it is present in the NEW PERSON file. If not, then itr returns the .01 field value from the NEW PERSON file in first m last forma

rpcs.name: VEJDWPB GET SCHED APPTS
rpcs.description: This will invoke SDA^VADPT.  It returns those appointments which were kept and all open future appointments depending upon the date range.
rpcs.returnDescription: Data passed to broker via the global ^TMP("VEJD",$J,"APT",#) Error messages: array(1) = "-1^Invalid patient DFN" if bad patient pointer array(1) =" -1^No appointments found  Returns array(#) = p1^p2^p3^p4 in reverse chronological order.  p1 :== external appointment date/time p2 :== external clinic n

rpcs.name: VEJDWPB GET VISITS/APPOINTMENT
rpcs.description: This will call the VEJDWPB GET SCHED APPTS rpc to get the scheduled appointments for the user inputted date range.  It then loops throughs the Visit file (#9000010) "AA" cross reference to find all visits.  It then uses the uses the IN5^VADPT to get the current admission. It then uses the uses the IN5^VADPT to get the current admission.
rpcs.returnDescription: It returns to the broker, via the global array ^TMP("VEJD",$J,"VSIT",#) p5 := date/time in internal fileman format p6 := location file (#44) ien 4. If no matching data was found then return -1^Invalid patient DFN -1^No visits or appointments found hospital location value (file 44) from the visit ien

rpcs.name: VEJDWPB GET SC CONDITIONS
rpcs.description: This will return a string indicating whether the service condition date/time and LOC must be the the hospital location data  If VST is passed, then APPT and LOC are optional. questions need to be asked: Agent Orange exposure Environmental Contaminant exposure Service Connected Military Sexual Trauma Head & Neck Cancer  If VST is not passed, then APPT must be the scheduling appointment
rpcs.returnDescription: This will return a string in the format p1^p2^p3^p4^p5^p6 where If invalid DFN passed, then error message returned, "-1^Invalid patient DFN  If the visit date cannot be determined, then error message returned "-1^No appointment/visit date pi = 1 or null   If pi=1 then ask appropriate yes/no question

rpcs.name: VEJDWPB ADD PROBLEM
rpcs.description: This will create a new entry in the problem list file using the D CREATE^GMPLUTL(.VEJDDATA,.VEJDRETN) call.
rpcs.returnDescription: If problem successfully added, returns 1^new IEN in problem list Otherwise, returns -1^Error message

rpcs.name: VEJDWPB UPDATE PROBLEM
rpcs.description: This will update an existing problem using the D UPDATE^GMPLUTL(.VEJDRETN,.VEJDDATA) call
rpcs.returnDescription: If update is successful, then returns 1^(data from gmplutl call) Otherwise, returns -1^(data from gmplutl call)

rpcs.name: VEJDWPB GET VISITS ONLY
rpcs.description: This loops through the Visit file (#9000010) AA cross reference to find all visits for a patient and date range
rpcs.returnDescription: The data is passed to the broker in the ^TMP("VEJD",$J,"VSIT",#) where Or it will return -1^No Patient DFN   or -1^No visits found ^TMP("VEJD",$J,"VSIT",#) = p1^p2^p3^p4^p5^p6 where p1 := A (if admission)  or  V (if visit) p2 := visit file pointer (#9000010) p3 := visit/admission external date/time

rpcs.name: VEJD DDR FINDER
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array in the following format:  1^MOUSE,MICKEY^000000001 2^MOUSE,MINNIE^000000002  but if VEJDFELD=".09^.01", you would see:  1^000000001^MOUSE,MICKEY 2^000000002^MOUSE,MINNIE  Array[0

rpcs.name: VEJD DDR GETS ENTRY DATA
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array with the following format:  Filenumber^IENS^FieldNumber^Result

rpcs.name: XWB EXAMPLE TRAP PARAMS
rpcs.description: This RPC is for testing use only. It calls the Error Trap to save off the symbol table for debuging.  The call accepts up to 9 parameters that are labeled P1 to P9.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return is the string: "Error Trap called to save symbol table"

rpcs.name: YTAPI NEW ASI
rpcs.description: This remote procedure allows new entries to be added to the ADDICTION SEVERITY INDEX file (#604).  Input required: none Output: YSDATA(1)=[DATA

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD CHECK
rpcs.description: Very similar to IMPORT^TIUSRVF, except does not save template fields. Resolves self referencing loops, and takes into account fields with the same name already saved.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array of named results and modified XML, separated by a line with "***".  The named results is in the format NAME^CODE^NEW_NAME, where NAME is the original name in the field, CODE indicates 0 or 1, where 1 means the field does not need to be renamed, and 0 indicates an error or that the f

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD LIST ADD
rpcs.description: Takes in the XML string, in the format XMLSET(1)=" <TEMPLATE_FIELDS>" and merges them with the global ^TMP("TIUFLDXML",$J).  If the subscript is 1, then it KILLs the global before it merges.  This routine is used so very large lists of fields can be processed without many calls to the database.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a "1" if successful; 0 otherwise.

rpcs.name: TIU FIELD LIST IMPORT
rpcs.description: Calls the import process for a pre-loaded (into ^TMP) list of template fields.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list of field names imported in the format NAME^RCODE^RENAME, where NAME is the desired field name, RCODE is 1 or 0 if the field was added/same or renamed (respectively), and RENAME is the actual saved name of the field if RCODE is 0.

rpcs.name: TIU SET DOCUMENT TEXT
rpcs.description: This RPC buffers the transmittal of text (i.e., the body of TIU Documents) from the Client to the Server. It allows documents of indefinite size to be filed, without risk of an allocate error on the M Server.
rpcs.returnDescription: Four '^'-piece scalar result formatted as follows:", ' If successful: <IEN in TIU DOCUMENT FILE>^<LAST_PAGE_RECEIVED>^<TOTAL_PAGES_EXPECTED>  If unsuccessful: 0^0^0^Explanatory text

rpcs.name: TIU SET ADMINISTRATIVE CLOSURE
rpcs.description: This remote procedure sets the file attributes necessary to close a document by administrative action (either manually or by scanning a paper', "document that doesn't require the signature of an author as a typical TIU", 'Document would).
rpcs.returnDescription: If successful, this RPC returns the IEN in the TIU DOCUMENT file (#8925) of the document that was closed.  Otherwise, it will return:  0^<Explanatory message>

rpcs.name: ORWPCE HNCOK
rpcs.description: Returns TRUE if the Head and/or Neck Cancer patches have been installed

rpcs.name: ORWDPS4 CPLST
rpcs.description: Get co-pay ralated questions

rpcs.name: ORWDPS4 CPINFO
rpcs.description: Save outpatient med order co-pay information.

rpcs.name: IBARXM TRANS BILL
rpcs.description: This rpc will receive data that a charge should be created for a pharmacy copayment that had previously not been charged due to the patient reaching their copayment cap amount. This RPC call does not send user data to remote side.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value will be the transaction ien in file 354.71, to indicate that billing was successful.  If not, then a -1 will be returned.

rpcs.name: VAFC REMOTE PDAT
rpcs.description: This Remote Procedure Call will return the text Patient MPI/PD Data Inquiry report to a remote site.
rpcs.returnDescription: This Remote Procedure Call will return the text Patient MPI/PD Data Inquiry report to a remote site.

rpcs.name: MAG4 POST PROCESSING
rpcs.description: This is call to perform any post processing action required on a newly saved image of this type.  post processing (for now) is determined by the MAG DESCRIPTIVE CAGETORIES file.  If this image points to an entry in that file that has Action turned ON, then the TAG^ROUTINE will be called with the Image IEN.
rpcs.returnDescription: 1^SUCCESSFUL 0^ERROR

rpcs.name: MAG4 VERSION CHECK
rpcs.description: This remote procedure checks the version of the Imaging client application. Depending on the version of the VistA Imaging KIDS install, the client application can be forced to display a warning and/or terminate.
rpcs.returnDescription: Result code and message are returned into the Results[0

rpcs.name: EC SPACEBAR
rpcs.description: This RPC would return the value equivalent to when the 'Spacebar and Return' ", 'keys are entering in the VISTA package.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RESULTS variable contains the IEN and the .01 field description.

rpcs.name: EC DIEDON
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a patient's date of death.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RPC returns either of the following pieces of data as output. 1. 0^Patient DFN is not defined 2. DOD internal fileman format^message when patient died 3. ^ [if patient is not dead

rpcs.name: ORQPT MAKE RPL
rpcs.description: Passes Team List IEN, creates a TMP file entry of patients based thereon, and receives a $J job number in return.
rpcs.returnDescription: Job Number ($J) to assist in subsequent reading of TMP global that was created.

rpcs.name: ORQPT READ RPL
rpcs.description: Passes global reference and other parameters, and receives a list of patients (up to 44 maximum) with IENs, for use in scrolling a Long List Box (LLB) componenet.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array of patients with their IENs for populating Long List Box (LLB) component.

rpcs.name: ORQPT KILL RPL
rpcs.description: This RPC is passed a ^TMP file root and $J (job number) and kills the ^TMP("ORRPL",$J global data based on the passed file root w/job number.

rpcs.name: ORWTIU GET SAVED CP FIELDS
rpcs.description: Given a TIU document of the clinical procedures class, return the author, title, cosigner, procedure summary code, date/time of procedure, and reference date, as stored on the server.
rpcs.returnDescription: AUTHOR^COSIGNER^PROCSUMMCODE^PROCDATE^DOCTITLE^REFDATE

rpcs.name: ORWPT LAST5 RPL
rpcs.description: Returns a list of patients matching the string of Last Name Initial_Last 4 SSN (Initial/Last 4 look-up based on Restricted Patient List).

rpcs.name: ORWPT FULLSSN RPL
rpcs.description: Given an SSN in the format 999999999(P), return a list of matching patients based on Restricted Patient List.

rpcs.name: ORWOR1 CHKDIG
rpcs.description: Returns true if an order requires a digital signature.

rpcs.name: ORWOR1 GETDTEXT
rpcs.description: Returns the external text of an existing order.

rpcs.name: ORWOR1 GETDSIG
rpcs.description: Returns the digital signature of an existing order.

rpcs.name: ORWOR1 SIG
rpcs.description: Returns 1 if signature gets stored.

rpcs.name: ORWCIRN RESTRICT
rpcs.description: This RPC checks for sensitive patients on a remote system.

rpcs.name: PSB GETORDERTAB
rpcs.description: Gives the client VDL information for the specified patient and time frame.
rpcs.returnDescription: Results[0

rpcs.name: PSB WARDLIST
rpcs.description: Returns a list of active wards that are available for the definition of IV parameters in the BCMA IV PARAMETERS file 53.66. Wards already in file 53.66 are returned with the type of IV PARAMETERS defined.
rpcs.returnDescription: Results(0)      =       ^1 the number of wards returned ^8 "S" if Syringe parameters in 53.66 Results(1,2...) =       ^1 the ward name ^2 "1" for BOLD (ward is defined in 53.66) "0" for NOBOLD (ward is not in 53.66) ^3 IEN number from file 53.66 ^4 "A" if Admixture parameters in 53.66 ^5 "C" if Chem

rpcs.name: PSB GETIVPAR
rpcs.description: Returns the IV parameters by IV type, as defined for a ward in file 53.66.
rpcs.returnDescription: A delimited string with the IV type and the defined parameters. ^7=Infusion Rate ^8=Med Route ^9=Schedule ^10=Admin Time ^11=Remarks ^12=Other Print Info ^13=Provider ^14=Start Date/Time ^15=Stop Date/Time ^16=Provider Comments 1=Warning, 2=No Warning, 3=Invalid.  ^1=IV type ^2=Additive ^3=Strength

rpcs.name: PSB DEVICE
rpcs.description: Allows user to select a Printer from the GUI.
rpcs.returnDescription: Results(1)=IEN;Name^DisplayName^Location^RMargin^PLength

rpcs.name: PSB PUTIVPAR
rpcs.description: Sets 53.66 or parameters file w/input from 53.66 if ward is not "ALL"
rpcs.returnDescription: The IV type - "A" for Additive ^6=Volume ^7=Infusion rate ^8=Med route ^9=Schedule ^10=Admin time ^11=Remarks ^12=Other Print Info ^13=Provider ^14=Start Date/time ^15=Provider comments "P" for Piggyback "H" for Hyperal "C" for Chemo "S" for Syringe ^2=Additive ^3=Strength ^4=Bottle ^5=Solution

rpcs.name: PSB BAG DETAIL

rpcs.description: Returns a list of allergies for a patient.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array of patient allergies.  Returned data is delimited by "^" and includes: allergen/reactant, reactions/symptoms (multiple symptoms/ reactions are possible - delimited by ";"), severity, allergy id (record number from the Patient Allergies file (#120.8).

rpcs.name: PSB GETPROVIDER
rpcs.description: Used to get a list of active providers.
rpcs.returnDescription: An array is returned in the format:  (0)     line count of array (1...n) IEN from VA(200^provider name

rpcs.name: PSB MOB DRUG LIST
rpcs.description: Used by the BCMA/CPRS Med Order Button to return an array of drug.
rpcs.returnDescription: Results[0

rpcs.name: PSB CPRS ORDER
rpcs.description: This RPC sets results of a Med Order Button transaction in a global for Inpatient Pharmacy to pick up.
rpcs.returnDescription: Results[0

rpcs.name: PSB NURS WARDLIST
rpcs.description: This will return a list of active ward from the NURS LOCATION, file 211.4.
rpcs.returnDescription: RESULTS(0)      =       Number of nodes returned. RESULTS(1...n)  =       IEN from file 211.4^NAME

rpcs.name: PSB MAXDAYS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the maximum number of days a user can view or print the MAH report. This parameter is set using CPRS and is call ORRP BCMA MAH.

rpcs.name: IBARXM QUERY SUPPRESS USER
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to query only the information for pharmacy co-payment billing that has happened for the given month/year. The RPC should be used only for queries, which will not allow the user to see information, because the user data will not be transmitted to remote system.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return array is in file 354.71 format.

rpcs.name: XQAL GUI ALERTS
rpcs.description: This is the RPC that handles the XUAlert component
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter is a global array containing the return values for the type of call.

rpcs.name: TIU IS THIS A CLINPROC?
rpcs.description: This RPC evaluates whether or not a Title is under the CLINICAL PROCEDURES Class.
rpcs.returnDescription: A boolean value is returned.  1 = True.  The Title is under CLINICAL PROCEDURES Class. 0 = False.  The Title is NOT under the CLINICAL PROCEDURES Class.

rpcs.name: TIU IDENTIFY CLINPROC CLASS
rpcs.description: This RPC gets the CLINICAL PROCEDURES TIU Document Definition file (#8925.1) IEN.
rpcs.returnDescription: A numeric value is returned.  Either the CLINICAL PROCEDURES TIU Document file (#8925.1) IEN or 0.

rpcs.name: TIU LONG LIST CLINPROC TITLES
rpcs.description: This RPC serves data to a longlist of selectable Titles for CLINICAL PROCEDURES.
rpcs.returnDescription: An array is returned.  This is an array of the 44 nearest titles to that indicated by the user in the direction passed by the longlist component.

rpcs.name: RG REMOTE HL7 TASK
rpcs.description: This remote procedure call will return the currently running Health Level Seven (HL7) tasks from a remote site to the Master Patient Index (MPI).
rpcs.returnDescription: This remote procedure call will return the currently running Health Level Seven (HL7) tasks from a remote site to the Master Patient Index (MPI).

rpcs.name: RG REM ACTIVITY
rpcs.description: This remote procedure call (RPC) returns Health Level Seven (HL7) message information and exception information for a patient.  The HL7 data is from the ADT/HL7 PIVOT (#391.71) file and exception date is from the CIRN HL7 EXCEPTION LOG (#991.1) file.
rpcs.returnDescription: ARRAY

rpcs.name: DGWPT DFLTSRC
rpcs.description: Return user's default patient list source.

rpcs.name: DGWPT TOP
rpcs.description: Returns the last selected patient by the defined user.

rpcs.name: DGWPT SELCHK
rpcs.description: Returns a 1 if the patient record is flagged as senstive, otherwise returns 0.

rpcs.name: DGWPT SAVDFLT
rpcs.description: Saves user's preference for default list source.

rpcs.name: DGWPT CLINRNG
rpcs.description: Returns a list of selectable options from which a user can choose a date range for appointments.

rpcs.name: DGWPT BYWARD
rpcs.description: Returns a list of patients currently residing on a specified ward location.

rpcs.name: DGWPT DIEDON
rpcs.description: Returns date of death if patient has expired.  Otherwise returns 0.

rpcs.name: DGWPT1 PRCARE

In the released version the Imaging BackGround Processor will be calling the Status Callback routine of CP.
