rpcs.description: This broker call files data entered in the Create Accident/Illness Report Option.

rpcs.name: OOPS EMPLOYEE DATA
rpcs.description: This call returns a listing of individuals in the PAID file #450 that match the Name or partial name entered.  It returns PAID fields 6, 10, 13, 16, 31, 32, 38, 186.1, 186.3, 186, 186.4, 458, 604.
rpcs.returnDescription: This return parameter, RESULTS, contains fields from the PAID Employee File (#450) that are needed in the ASISTS Accident Report File (#2260).  An example of this data is as follows:  14189^WILBUR,CHARLES^^***-**-****^HIGH SCHOOL + ADDITIONAL TRAINING PROGR AM (CO^13^NURSING ASSISTANT^0621^M^JUN 27,

rpcs.name: OOPS NEW PERSON DATA
rpcs.description: This call returns the name of new person, sex, DOB, and SSN of the individual whose name is passed in the input parameter.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter contains the name of new person, sex, DOB, and SSN if an entry is found in the New Person File (#200).  The return paramter format is: 123456814^TURNER,JAMES^MALE^^333999888^INFORMATION SYSTEMS CENTER

rpcs.name: OOPS GET 2260 DATA
rpcs.description: This broker call will return data from ASISTS file 2260. The fields (in the array) returned will vary depending on the input parameter. One field of the input parameter is FORM. If the claim is for a 2162, all the fields associated with "2162" nodes along with zero nodes will be returned. If the form is either CA1 or CA2 all associated nodes will be returned in addition to the zero and "CA" nodes.

rpcs.name: OOPS REPLACE DATE/TIME
rpcs.description: This call assures the date and/or time is a valid and reformats it.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, either returns a data in a valid Fileman format or returns an error message indicating that the date passed in the broker call was invalid and there was a "DATE ERROR".

rpcs.name: OOPS GET CKRANGE
rpcs.description: This broker call is used to return an array which holds the integer values stored in the field included in the input parameter. Currently in ASISTS, the fields that are evaluated/returned via this broker are:  140 Regular Work Schedule for CA1 244 Regular Work Schedule for CA2
rpcs.returnDescription: Return parameter RESULTS is an array containing the integers used to indicate a work schedule, ie. 2,3,4,5,6.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET WITNESSES
rpcs.description: This broker call with take and IEN as input and retrieve witness information and associated comments from the ASISTS 2260 file.
rpcs.returnDescription: The Return parameter is an Array containing the Witness information (sometimes multiple entries ) and associated comments.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET DEFAULT MD
rpcs.description: This call will return the Default Medical Doctor information based on an IEN in the ASISTS 2260 file being passed in. The information returned is from the file 2262.
rpcs.returnDescription: The results array will return data in the following format.   Results(0)="1^Valid Results" or "0^No Valid Results Results(1)=PROVIDER NAME Results(2)=PROVIDER ADDRESS Results(3)=PROVIDER CITY Results(4)=PROVIDER STATE Results(5)=PROVIDER ZIP CODE Results(6)=PROVIDER TITLE

rpcs.name: OOPS GET SUPERVISOR
rpcs.description: This call looks up the Supervisor from the new person file.  The IEN of the claim is passed in to make sure that the person involved is not the supervisor.
rpcs.returnDescription: This return parameter, RESULTS, contains a list of indiviuals found in the New Person File (#200) based on the input parameter, NAME.  The format for this parameter is: IEN200^NAME^SSN^SERVICE

rpcs.name: OOPS CREATE AMENDMENT
rpcs.description: Files an Amendment for the selected case in ASISTS.
rpcs.returnDescription: The new ASISTS case number is returned.

rpcs.name: OOPS EDIT 2260
rpcs.description: This broker call passes in ASISTS data and files it in the ASISTS Accident Reporting File (#2260).
rpcs.returnDescription: Array indicating the success or failure of the update to the 2260 file.

rpcs.name: OOPS CHANGE CASE STATUS
rpcs.description: This broker call passes in the ASISTS IEN, new case status and if the case status is deleted the reason for deletion.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, contains a message indicating the new case number, if changed.  The message is: Case Status has been changed ', "to: 'case #'.

rpcs.name: OOPS REPLACE WP
rpcs.description: This call will replace Word Processing fields based on input paramters indicating the file, field and IEN of the record that will be changed. Another input parameter contains the data via a list or pointer that will be placed into the "new" word processing fields.
rpcs.returnDescription: This return parameter does not contain any data or messages back to the client system.  The return parameter is required in all broker calls and is therefore present in this call.

rpcs.name: OOPS VALIDATE TIME
rpcs.description: This broker call will compare two dates (which are part of the input) based on the 3rd input paramater which is a flag indicating which type of compare should be done.  The RESULTS output indicates whether the dates pass (VALID DATE) or fail (DATE ERROR) the compare.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, indicates if the compare was good (VALID DATE) or bad (DATE ERROR).

rpcs.name: OOPS GET DATA
rpcs.description: This broker call returns the data in the ASISTS Accident Reporting File (#2260) for fields that are 1) Set of Codes, 2) Set of Codes Multiple, 3) Pointer to a file, and 4) Pointer to a file multiple.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RESULTS array will contain the data that was requested.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET UNION
rpcs.description: This call retrieves a listing of all Unions.
rpcs.returnDescription: The 0 node of the ASISTS UNION INFORMATION File (#2263.7) is returned in the results array.  This node contains the Union Name, Acronym, and Union Representative.  The format of the return parameter is: AMERICAN FED OF CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION^AFLCIO^2440^HOWELL,LYNN^2

rpcs.name: OOPS DELETE UNION
rpcs.description: This broker call will delete an entry in the Union File (#2263.7).
rpcs.returnDescription: A success/failure message will be passed back to the client.

rpcs.name: OOPS PUT UNION
rpcs.description: This call will either file a new Union record if there is no internal record number passed back or it will edit an existing union record if the internal record number is present and passed back.
rpcs.returnDescription: The results parameter will contain a status message regarding the filing of the data.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET SITE PARAMETER
rpcs.description: This broker call returns the Site Parameter Name, IEN and the DOL District Office and is used to obtain the entries in the Station multiple.
rpcs.returnDescription: This broker call returns the Site Parameter Name, IEN, and DOL District Office and is used to obtain the entries in the Station multiple.

rpcs.name: OOPS SET FIELD
rpcs.description: This broker call will set a single field in file 2260.
rpcs.returnDescription: The output (RESULTS) will contain a message indicating the results of the attempted update. "UPDATE FAILED" indicates there was an error/failure. UPDATE COMPLETE" indicates the update was valid.

rpcs.name: OOPS DELETE SITEPAR STATION
rpcs.description: This broker call deletes the Station Subfile for the IEN passed into it.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, contains a message indicating whether the deletion was successful (Record successfully deleted) or was not (Deletion did not occur) successful.

rpcs.name: OOPS EDIT SITEPAR STATION
rpcs.description: This broker call will either add a new Station subfile to the Site parameter file or will allow for editing an existing Station subfile.  If the Station IEN is passed in the INPUT parameter, the record will be edited.  If a Station IEN is not passed in the input parameter, a new record will be created.
rpcs.returnDescription: This return parameter, RESULTS, contains several messages indicating the status of filing the edits made to the Site Parameter file.  These messages include: Missing Record Identifiers, Cannot file Missing Station, Cannot continue Missing Station, cannot file Update Successful Update was not Success

rpcs.name: OOPS DELETE WITNESS
rpcs.description: This broker call will delete witness information from the "CA1W" node in file 2260 based on the IEN and sub file id passed in.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return array will contain a status message for the deletion process and whether it was successful (SUCCESSFULLY DELETED) or not (DELETION FAILED) sucessful.

rpcs.name: OOPS WITNESS CREATE
rpcs.description: This broker call will add a new Witness record for a case in the ASISTS Accident Reporting File (#2260).  It is called by the CA1 form in Delphi.
rpcs.returnDescription: This will be a string, either "WITNESS CREATION FAILED" indicating the witness record was not created, or "WITNESS CREATION SUCCESSFUL" indicating success.

rpcs.name: OOPS WITNESS EDIT
rpcs.description: This broker call files any changes entered on an existing Witness.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, contains a message indicating whether the edits were successfully made (EDIT SUCCESSFULL) or not (EDIT FAILED) successfully made.

rpcs.name: OOPS WITNESS DELETE
rpcs.description: The broker call will remove witness information (stored in node "CA1W") from the 2260 file.

rpcs.name: OOPS EDIT SITE PARAMETER
rpcs.description: This broker call files changes to the Site Parameter File (#2262) name and District Office, if made.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, contains a message indicating whether changes made to the top level record were successful (Update Site data Successful) or were not (Update Site data was NOT Successful) successful.

rpcs.name: OOPS APPROVE SIGN FOR EMPLOYEE
rpcs.description: This broker call will determine whether the individual can approve the', "Workers' Comp person being able to sign for the employee if accessed from the", 'Safety or Employee Health menus.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RESULTS(0) node in the array will contain a 1 or 0.  If a 1, then the', "individual approving the Workers' Comp signing for the Employee is Ok, otherwise", 'the signature form will not be available.

rpcs.name: OOPS VALIDATE AND SIGN
rpcs.description: This broker call handles the validation of data prior to a user entering their electronic signature code.  It also validates the electronic signature code and then assures that the users electronic signature code, the date/time of signature, and the users DUZ is filed in the Vista database.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RESULTS array will contain different message that indicate any problems or issues detected during the signing process.  The following messages are possible: User not Authorized to sign form Claim cannot be signed until the Bill of Rights Statement is understood No Signature Entered No Electronic

rpcs.name: OOPS REPLACE MULTIPLE
rpcs.description: This procedure is for updating entries in a multiple, in 2 stages. Stage 1 deletes all current entries, step 2 creates new entries using data passed back from the GUI form.

rpcs.name: OOPS UNION CONSENT
rpcs.description: This broker call will send the Bulletin to the union representative selected. Note: a ASISTS legacy routine is called from this broker call to send the bulletin.
rpcs.returnDescription: The output parameter, RESULTS(0) will contain text indicating if the bulletin was sent, "BULLETIN SENT" or failed, "BULLETIN FAILED".

rpcs.name: OOPS GET STATION INFORMATION
rpcs.description: This broker call returns the Address, City, State and Zip code for a station stored in DIC(4 Institution file.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS contains the Street Address, City, State and Zip code for the Station specified in the input parameter. It is in the format ST ADDR^CITY^STATE^ZIP if an entry is found in the Institution file (^DIC(4)).  If the input parameter, IEN, for the station is invalid or an entr

rpcs.name: OOPS MANUAL XMIT DATA
rpcs.description: This broker call is the front end for calling the routine to transmit data to the AAC for DOL (CA1 and CA2s) or to transmit data to the National Database (NDB).
rpcs.returnDescription: The Results parameter will contain the status message of the queue.  If not', "successful, it will be 'ERROR', if successful it will be 'SUCCESSFULLY QUEUED'.

rpcs.name: OOPS INCIDENT REPORT
rpcs.description: This broker call returns the data for creating the Type of Incident reports. This report provides information on different Types of incidents.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RESULTS parameter will return data in the array.

rpcs.name: OOPS WCEDIT
rpcs.description: This call clears the Supervisor signature and related fields if the Workers Compensation specialist has edited fields INJURED PERFORMING DUTY (#146), NOT INJURED PERFORMING JOB (#147), INJURY CAUSED BY EMPLOYEE (#148), CAUSED BY EMPLOYEE EXPLAIN (#149), SUPERVISOR NOT AGREE EXPLAN (#164), or REASON AGENCY CONTROVERTS COP (#165) after the Supervisor signed.  It also ', "sets the field WORKER'S COMP EDIT (#199) so the required bulletin will ", 'be sent.
rpcs.returnDescription: The RESULTS parameter will contain the name of the person who signed as the Supervisor.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET OSHA DATA
rpcs.description: This broker call returns the data needed to produce the Log of Federal Occupational Injuries and Illnesses Report.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter will contain the data, delimited by ^, for the data to be displayed in the report.  The return parameter name is RESULTS.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET PRT ACC STATUS RPT
rpcs.description: This broker call retrieves the data required to generate the PRINT ACCIDENT REPORT STATUS Report.
rpcs.returnDescription: This broker call returns the ASISTS data to produce the PRINT ACCIDENT STATUS Report.

rpcs.name: OOPS CLEAR SIGNATURE
rpcs.description: This broker call clears the signature from the ASISTS case for Form and discipline (calling menu).

rpcs.name: OOPS NEEDLESTICK LOG
rpcs.description: This broker call retrieves the data necessary to produce the Log of Needlestick report.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter is a global array containing all the fields needed to produce the report.  The data is returned in the global ^TMP($J,"NS").

rpcs.name: OOPS GET NOI CODE
rpcs.description: This broker call returns the listing of NOI codes.  If the claim is a CA1 then only codes begining with T are returned, if the claim is a CA2 only codes that do not begin with a T are returned.
rpcs.returnDescription: The results array will return a list of NOI Descriptions with the code.

rpcs.name: OOPS SET RECORD LOCK
rpcs.description: This broker call attempt to lock the record and if not successful, returns a message indicating that the record is in use by another user.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter will contain a status message concerning the lock.

rpcs.name: OOPS RELEASE RECORD LOCK
rpcs.description: This broker call will release the previously locked ASISTS record.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter will contain a status message relating to clearing the lock.

rpcs.name: OOPS GET ASISTS CASE
rpcs.description: This broker call completes a lookup on the ASISTS Accident Reporting file and returns a list of valid ASISTS cases for selection.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter contains a list of all valid claims for this person.

rpcs.name: OOPS CHECK PAID EMP DATA
rpcs.description: This broker call checks to make sure that if the PERSONNEL STATUS (field #2) is an employee and the Pay Plan is equal to "OT" that the Grade (#16), Step (#17), and Retirement (#60) fields contain valid responses.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return parameter, RESULTS, contains a message indicating whether the PAID data for the ASISTS claim being validated is valid (Valid) or invalid (Invalid data in claim).

rpcs.name: OOPS GET SINGLE FIELD
rpcs.description: This broker call will return a single data element from the file and field that is passed in as a parameter.
rpcs.returnDescription: The external value of the data element extracted from the file and field passed in the second parameter will be returned.

rpcs.name: MAGJ LOGOFF
rpcs.description: Capture the Logoff event for the Imaging Session file.
rpcs.returnDescription: Always returns "1"

rpcs.name: MPIF SEEDING UPDATE
rpcs.description: Setting number of entries to be sent during seeding.
rpcs.returnDescription: Successful - 1 Unsuccessful - 0

rpcs.name: MPIF SEEDING STATS
rpcs.description: This RPC will return the stats on the seeding process, including when the next seeding job is scheduled to run
rpcs.returnDescription: RETURN(1)= file 984.8 zero node RETURN(2)= when MPIF SEEDING TASK is scheduled to run next RETURN(3)= count of how many ICNs are remaining to be seeded.

rpcs.name: ORRHCR RPTLST
rpcs.description: Returns the list of reports that are available to a specific user.
rpcs.returnDescription: A list of reports where:  LST(n)=IEN^DisplayName

rpcs.name: ORRHCR RPTDEF
rpcs.description: Returns the name-value pairs the represent the definition of a report.
rpcs.returnDescription: Name-Value pairs the represent the query for the report and the formatting of the report.

rpcs.name: ORRHCU ID2EXT
rpcs.description: Returns the external name of an entry given the internal number.
rpcs.returnDescription: A list of resolved internal entry numbers where LST(n)=IEN^NAME

rpcs.name: ORRHCO OISETS
rpcs.description: Returns the list of sets available for listing orderable items.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list where LST(n)=ShortName^DisplayGroupName

rpcs.name: ORRHCO ORDITM
rpcs.description: Returns a subset of orderable items for use in a long list box.
rpcs.returnDescription: List in the format:  LST(n)=IEN^DisplayName or:  LST(n)=IEN^Mnemonic  <DisplayName>

rpcs.name: ORRHCT DOCCLS
rpcs.description: Returns a list of document classes.
rpcs.returnDescription: Document Classes:  IEN^DocumentClass

rpcs.name: ORRHCO ORDSTS
rpcs.description: Returns a list of order statuses
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list:  LST(n)=IEN^StatusName

rpcs.name: ORRHCO SIGNSTS
rpcs.description: Returns a list of signature statuses
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list where:  LST(n)=Code^SignatureStatusName

rpcs.name: ORRHCT DOCSTS
rpcs.description: Returns a list of document statuses.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list where LST(n)=IEN^StatusName

rpcs.name: ORRHCU BYREG
rpcs.description: Returns a list of patients listed in a patient registry.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list of patients:  LST(n)=DFN^PatientName

rpcs.name: ORRHCR USRRPT
rpcs.description: Returns the IEN of an entry in CPRS QUERY DEFINITION given the display text of the entry.  The entry must be a user-owned report.
rpcs.returnDescription: Internal Entry Number of the report that was looked up.

rpcs.name: ORRHCR SAVDEF
rpcs.description: Accepts a set of name-value pairs that describe a newly created custom report.  This creates a new entry in CPRS QUERY DEFINITION.
rpcs.returnDescription: The IEN of the newly created report entry.

rpcs.name: ORRHCU DFLDS
rpcs.description: Returns a list of name-value pairs that represent the display fields that are available based on the search items selected.
rpcs.returnDescription: A list of name-value pairs that represent the columns available to the user for selection.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ SETUP
rpcs.description: Sets up in ^TMP("ORRHCQ",$J) the parameters and constraints that will be used for the query.
rpcs.returnDescription: Always returns true.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ SORTBY
rpcs.description: Causes the query results that are in ^TMP("ORRHCQD",$J) to be sorted on a particular column.
rpcs.returnDescription: The sequence number of the first item to display.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ SUBDTA
rpcs.description: Returns name-value pairs that represent columns for a subset of records. The list view in the GUI posts an event for which records it needs to display.  This call obtains the data for those records.
rpcs.returnDescription: Name-value pairs that represent each cell for the range of rows returned.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ DETAIL
rpcs.description: Returns the text to display for the details of an order.
rpcs.returnDescription: Word processing text describing order details.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ PTINFO
rpcs.description: Returns patient identifying information to display in the bar above the detailed display.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns  PatientName^SSN^Ward^Room-Bed

rpcs.name: ORRHCR OWNED
rpcs.description: Returns true if the current user owns this report.
rpcs.returnDescription: 1 if the user owns the report, otherwise 0.

rpcs.name: ORRHCR DELETE
rpcs.description: Deletes a user-owned report
rpcs.returnDescription: none

rpcs.name: ORRHCU COLTYP
rpcs.description: Returns the sort types for the list of columns passed in.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list where LST(n)=ColumnName^SortType

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ CLEAR
rpcs.description: Cancels the query and cleans up the TMP globals.
rpcs.returnDescription: Always returns true.

rpcs.name: ORRHCU REGLST
rpcs.description: Returns a list of patient registries.
rpcs.returnDescription: List of the names of available registries.

rpcs.name: ORRHCU REGNAM
rpcs.description: Returns the full name of a registry given the abbreviated name.
rpcs.returnDescription: The full registry name.

rpcs.name: ORRHCR CTPLST
rpcs.description: Returns a list of all criteria that are available as query constraints and the parent search items each criterion is associated with.
rpcs.returnDescription: A list where:  LST(n)=Parent Tag = Child Tag

rpcs.name: ORRHCR TAGDEF
rpcs.description: Obtains a query definition based on the value of the TAG field.
rpcs.returnDescription: The name value pairs that represent a query definition.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ QRYITR
rpcs.description: Executes the query for a patient.  An iterator is passed in, in the format:  ListSource Subscript ^ DFN ^ Item#  The value returned includes the records found and the next iterator:  PtSearched ^ RecordCount ^ ListSource Subscript ^ NextDFN ^ Next Item#
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the total number of records found for the patient.  The actual query results are stored in ^TMP("ORRHCQD",$J).

rpcs.name: RMIM FIM PARAMETER
rpcs.description: This remote procedure will return the local site parameters in an array.  There are no input parameters.

rpcs.name: RMIM GET PATIENT DME
rpcs.description: This remote procedure will return a list of durable medical equipment will be followed by a ^ and a string of text describing the error.  The other elements of the array will be constructed as follows:  SHORT ITEM DESCRIPTION ^ TOTAL COST which has been issued to a patient within a date range.  Required input consists of a single parameter representing the internal entry number of a patient from file #2, and two dates in FileMan format. The three pieces will be delimited by the ^.  The first ele
rpcs.returnDescription: ITEM DESCRIPTION ^ TOTAL COST

rpcs.name: RMIM PATIENT LOOKUP
rpcs.description: Input parameter is any string that a user would normally enter to look up a patient.  Output is an array of DFN^PATIENT NAME^SSN^DOB.

rpcs.name: RMIM PATIENT INFO
rpcs.description: Input parameter is PATIENT DFN.  Output parameter is a ^ delimited string as follows: DFN^PATIENT NAME^SSN^DOB^AGE^SEX^MARITAL STATUS^ACTIVE DUTY^ CITY^STATE^ZIP^COUNTY^TELEPHONE

rpcs.name: RMIM CONSULT LIST
rpcs.description: Returns a list of consult requests for a patient within optional date range and optional service.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array of consult requests in the form: Consult id^date/time of request^status^consulting service^procedure

rpcs.name: ANRV CREATE OUTCOME
rpcs.description: Creates new Outcome Record.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a 1 if update occurs.

rpcs.name: ANRV GET OUTCOME TEXT
rpcs.description: Returns subrecord text.
rpcs.returnDescription: Subrecord text.

rpcs.name: ANRV GET PT OUTCOMES
rpcs.description: Returns top level IEN and sub record IEN plus statuses.

rpcs.name: ANRV GET PTALL
rpcs.description: Returns a list of all patients beginning with(x), from(x), to(x) direction.

rpcs.name: ANRV GET PTLAST5
rpcs.description: Returns a list of patients using the last 5 format for inquiry e.g. Z9999.

rpcs.name: ANRV PTINFO CORE
rpcs.description: Returns patient information for verification.  Must be changed eventually to include current Sensitive Patient Check.(TO DO).

rpcs.name: ANRV TANRVMESSAGE
rpcs.description: Create's and sends completed VIST Outcomes to the national mailgroup to ", 'populate the national database.

rpcs.name: ANRV SET RECORD STATUS
rpcs.description: Updates the status of the top level Outcome record.

rpcs.name: ANRV GUI PARAMETER
rpcs.description: Manages the ANRV system parameters.

rpcs.name: ANRV TANRVUSER
rpcs.description: Manages all User calls.

rpcs.name: ANRV FULLSSN
rpcs.description: Given an SSN in the format 999999999(P), return a list of matching patients. patients.

rpcs.name: IBCN INSURANCE QUERY
rpcs.description: This does a remote query on the insurance information.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return is in the following format: var("S",COB sequence,x) =: (null) as an xref for COB  -- output var(0)   =: number of entries insurance multiple var(x,0) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,0) var(x,1) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,1) var(x,2) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,2) var(x,3) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,3) var(x,4) =: ^dpt(dfn,.31

rpcs.name: IBCN INSURANCE QUERY TASK
rpcs.description: This does a remote query on the insurance information. This is the one that used during tasked jobs to avoid user data exchange.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return is in the following format: var("S",COB sequence,x) =: (null) as an xref for COB  -- output var(0)   =: number of entries insurance multiple var(x,0) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,0) var(x,1) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,1) var(x,2) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,2) var(x,3) =: ^dpt(dfn,.312,x,3) var(x,4) =: ^dpt(dfn,.31

rpcs.name: TIU USER INACTIVE?
rpcs.description: RPC evaluates user's DIUSER status and termination status when selected.", 'Returns 0 if active 1 if inactive
rpcs.returnDescription: 1 if the user is inactive, 0 if active.

rpcs.name: TIU ONE VISIT NOTE?
rpcs.description: Boolean RPC to evaulate if note has a corresponding visit.
rpcs.returnDescription: Boolean value; 1 if limited to one visit, 0 if any.

rpcs.name: TIU HAS AUTHOR SIGNED?
rpcs.description: Boolean RPC returns a value of 0 if the author has not signed and the user attempting to sign is the expected co-signer.  Returns a 1 if the author has signed or the user attempting to sign is NOT the expected co-signer.
rpcs.returnDescription: Boolean Value: 0 if author has NOT signed AND user is EC 1 if author HAS signed OR user is NOT EC 1 if author HAS signed OR user is NOT EC

rpcs.name: PSA UPLOAD
rpcs.description: This is the primary entry point for the Drug Accountability Upload GUI.

rpcs.name: RMPR TRANS LIST
rpcs.description: GUI 2319 Appliance Transaction List Tab 4.

rpcs.name: RMPR PT DEM
rpcs.description: This procedure supplies the Patient Demographic data for RESULTS(3)=DOB RESULTS(4)=AGE RESULTS(5)=SEX RESULTS(6)=DOD RESULTS(7)=RACE RESULTS(8)=RELIGION RESULTS(9)=MARRITAL STATUS RESULTS(11)=PRIMARY LONG ID RESULTS(12)=PRIMARY SHORT ID RESULTS(20)=FIRST LINE ST ADDRESS TAB 1 of the GUI 2319 RESULTS(21)=CITY RESULTS(22)=STATE RESULTS(23)=ZIP RESULTS(24)=COUNTY RESULTS(25)=PHONE RESULTS(26)=ZIP+4 RESULTS(27)=NOK RESULTS(28)=NOK FIRST LINE ADDRESS RESULTS(29)=NOK CITY RESULTS(30)=NOK STATE Same ro

rpcs.description: GUI 2319 Patient Demographics Tab 1. rpcs.name: RMPR TRANS HISA
rpcs.description: GUI 2319 HISA information Tab 7.  PASS IEN OF FILE 668.  RETURNED RESUTLS

rpcs.name: RMPR TRANS HOME O2
rpcs.description: GUI 2319 Home Oxygen tab 8.

rpcs.name: RMPR CRITICAL COM
rpcs.description: GUI 2319 Critical Comment Tab 6.  PASS IEN OF FILE 668  RETURN RESULTS ARRAY FROM FILE 665 CRITICAL COMMENTS FIELD.  RESUTLS()=ONE LINE AT A TIME OF THE WORD PROCESSING FIELD. ONE LINE TO INFINITE.

rpcs.name: RMPR DIS LIST
rpcs.description: GUI 2319 MAS DISABILITY AND PERCENT.  PASS IEN OF FILE 668.  RETURN RESULTS FROM FILE #2.  RESULTS()=DISABILITY DESCRIPTION^%^SC YESorNO. One line or multiple lines.

rpcs.name: ACKQAUD1

rpcs.returnDescription: See routine ACKQAG01 for a complete description of the return array. 9=tester name for second audiogram 10=age of patient at second audiogram 11=title of tester for second audiogram ACKQARR(ctr) pieces for each all X values 1=X Value(Hz) being tested 2=ACKQI - internal record number in 509850.9 3=ea

rpcs.name: ACKQAUD2
rpcs.description: Input the IEN of the 509850.9 file entry as the second parameter. Input the DFN as the third parameter, array by reference as first returns a subscripted array of data values for this audiogram data for the patient. Currently only uses 0 node for information. Used in the Enter/Edit Audiogram Delphi program
rpcs.returnDescription: Subscripted array of values for various Hz. See routine ACKQAG02 for a complete description of the return array.

rpcs.name: ACKQROES
rpcs.description: This is the RPC used to setup and send to the DDC, the signed audiometric data file entry to the DDC from the application Audiogram Enter/Edit (AKCQROES3E.EXE). It is triggered by the saving of a signed entry in 509850.9.  The RPC calls routine START^ACKQAG03 which in turn calls EN^ACKQAG04. The VADPT utilities are also used.
rpcs.returnDescription: It returns a literal containing the local confirmation msg number, the msg number sent to the DDC, the error number, & the error msg.

rpcs.description: Call FIND^DIC to get a list of matches on the file for the input value

rpcs.description: This calls the Fileman lister, LIST^DIC to get a list of records matching the input value
rpcs.returnDescription: If successful, return array will be in the following order: File Number^IEN^Field Number^Text

rpcs.description: This will allow you to add or delete an entry to file 19621.  When you use the VistA Imaging import an image API, you are returned a queue number.  This number means that VistA Imaging background processor accepted your request and place it a queue and will process it in due time.  After you get this successful queue message you should immediately file that data through this RPC.  This file is to allow communication between the VistA Imaging background processor and the DSS Document Management s
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a string: If successfully added return 1^message^TRANID If successfully deleted return 1^message If unsuccessful return -1^error message

rpcs.name: DSIC GET SC CONDITIONS
rpcs.description: This will return a string indicating whether the service condition Proj 112/SHAD  If VST is not passed, then APPT must be the scheduling appointment date/time and LOC must be the the hospital location data  If VST is passed, then APPT and LOC are optional. questions need to be asked: Agent Orange exposure Environmental Contaminant exposure Ionizing Radiation Exposure Service Connected Military Sexual Trauma Head & Neck Cancer Combat Veteran
rpcs.returnDescription: This will return a string in the format p1^p2^...^pn where p8 = ask PROJ 112/SHAD  If invalid DFN passed, then error message returned, "-1^Invalid patient DFN  If the visit date cannot be determined, then error message returned "-1^No appointment/visit date pn = 1 or null   If pn=1 then ask appropri

rpcs.name: DSIC GET SCHED APPTS
rpcs.description: This will invoke SDA^VADPT.  It returns those appointments which were kept and all open future appointments depending upon the date range.
rpcs.returnDescription: Data passed to broker via the global ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"APT",#) Error messages: array(1) = "-1^Invalid patient DFN" if bad patient pointer array(1) =" -1^No appointments found  Returns array(#) = p1^p2^p3^p4 in reverse chronological order.  p1 :== external appointment date/time p2 :== external clinic n

rpcs.name: DSIC GET VISITS/APPOINTMENT
rpcs.description: This will call the DSIC GET SCHED APPTS rpc to get the scheduled appointments for the user inputted date range.  It uses SELECTED^VSIT to find all visits.  It uses IN5^VADPT to get the current admission.  It will also get historical admission data, but manipulate the hospital location pointer values to the Visit LOCATION (#9999999.06) file, that points to the INSTITUTION (#4) file.
rpcs.returnDescription: It returns to the broker, via the global array ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"VSIT",#) p5 := date/time in internal fileman format p6 := location file (#44) ien  p4 & p6 may be entries from the INSTITUTION (#4) file.  The SELECTED^VSIT API has different meanings for historical hospitalizations versus Outpatient Vis

rpcs.name: DSIC GET VISITS ONLY
rpcs.description: Using SELECTED^VSIT this retrieves all visits for the date range.
rpcs.returnDescription: The data is passed to the broker in the ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"VSIT",#) where Or it will return -1^No Patient DFN   or -1^No visits found ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"VSIT",#) = p1^p2^p3^p4^p5^p6 where p1 := A (if admission)  or  V (if visit) p2 := visit file pointer (#9000010) p3 := visit/admission external date/time

If problems encountered, return the word ERR NOTES: -1 is a valid return value for an internal Delphi date.time  Thus for any problems encou

rpcs.name: DSIC XPD PATCH

If the KIDS install does not successfully run to completion, and that site does not rerun the KIDS install, then this patch data m

rpcs.name: DSIC XPD VERSION

As such it will only work for an application that has an associated PACKAGE file entry.  It will work for DSI applications but not for VEJD.

So you can mix and match parameters, entities, instances, and actions.

rpcs.name: DSIC XIP
rpcs.description: This returns address demographics for a 5 or 9 digit zip code The return value is for the primary location associated with the ZIP code. if an error:  -1 ^ ERROR MESSAGE if OK:   Input ZIP code ^ city ^ state ^ county ^ FIPS county code the return is for the primary location associated with the ZIP code.
rpcs.returnDescription: On error, return -1^error message Else return p1^p2^p3^p4^p5^p6^p7^p8  where p1 = inputted zip code     p5 = FIPS county code p2 = city                  p6 = state abbreviation p3 = state (full name)     p7 = ptr to STATE file (#5) p4 = county (full name)    p8 = ptr to COUNTY CODE file (#5.13)

rpcs.name: DSIC ICD9 GET LIST
rpcs.description: This will return a list of active ICD9 codes for a lookup value.
rpcs.returnDescription: This returns a list of active diagnosis codes matching the lookup value. n = 0,1,2,3,4,....   On error or problems, List[0

rpcs.name: DSIC PX GET VISIT INFO
rpcs.description: This RPC is a wrapper around the ENCEVENT^PXKENC api.  It does not return all the data that the PX api does.  It returns a subset of that data. The PX api is designed to return all the visit date in the various files for a VISIT ien.  See routine for more detailed description.
rpcs.returnDescription: On error return List[0

rpcs.name: DSIC DM CHECK
rpcs.description: This will check whether or not the current VistA system supports VistA Imagings import API.
rpcs.returnDescription: If VistA Imaging supports import API, then return 1 Else return -1^error message

rpcs.name: DSIC PX PRIMARY
rpcs.description: For a VISIT ien this will return the primary PCE diagnosis and the primary PCE provider.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return p1^p2^p3^p4  where In that case the return value is (all values are for primary pce: icd9 ien ^ icd9 code ^ icd9 short desc ^ duz ^ name p1 = primary pce diagnosis short description p2 = primary pce diagnosis code p3 = primary pce provider duz p4 = primary pce provider name (first m last form

rpcs.name: DSIC VA DEFAULT SITE
rpcs.description: This will return the name and station number for a medical center division.  Optional input parameters are allowed.  However, if not passed then return the data for the primary division (or institution) as of TODAY.
rpcs.returnDescription: On error, return -1^error message Else return p1^p2^p3^p4  where p1 = pointer to Institution file (#4) p2 = Institution file name p3 = Station number p4 = pointer to Medical Center Division file (#40.8) for that division which was the primary division as of that date.

rpcs.name: DSIC FM GET FIELD ATTRIB
rpcs.description: This will return the inputted field attributes for a file. DELETE ACCESS DESCRIPTION FIELD LENGTH GLOBAL SUBSCRIPT LOCATION HELP-PROMPT INPUT TRANSFORM LABEL MULTIPLE-VALUED OUTPUT TRANSFORM POINTER  READ ACCESS SOURCE SPECIFIER TECHNICAL DESCRIPTION TITLE TYPE WRITE ACCESS XECUTABLE HELP As of 7/29/2003, these attributes are available: AUDIT AUDIT CONDITION COMPUTE ALGORITHM COMPUTED FIELDS USED DATE FIELD LAST EDITED DECIMAL DEFAULT
rpcs.returnDescription: If problems or error, return List[0

rpcs.name: DSIC FM EXTERNAL
rpcs.description: This will convert an internal formatted data to external format.  This differs from the GET Fileman calls in that a record ien is not required. However, it is the responsibility of the developer to make sure that for the particular file (or subfile) and field that the DD will output the data without an internal number (e.g., DA is not defined).
rpcs.returnDescription: Return the external value for that file and field or -1^message

rpcs.name: DSIC FM VERIFY FILE
rpcs.description: This will verify whether or not a file exists.
rpcs.returnDescription: If the file exists, return the file number. Else return -1^message

rpcs.name: DSIC FM VERIFY FIELD
rpcs.description: This will verify whether or not a field exists in a file (or subfile).
rpcs.returnDescription: If the field exists, return the field number. Else return -1^error message

rpcs.name: DSIC WARD PTF SPECIALTY
rpcs.description: For a ward name (or a pointer to the WARD file), return that ward's", '  ptf specialty code ^ specialty name ^ specialty service
rpcs.returnDescription: Return ptf specialty code ^ specialty name ^ specialty service  If problems return -1^message

rpcs.name: DSIC DPT INP INFO
rpcs.description: This with return information about an admission event.  This RPC is a wrapper around the IN5^VADPT API.  If you pass in a date, then the data related to that date and admission will be returned,  If no date is passed, then get inpatient status as of NOW.  For more detailed description, see IN^DSICDPT routine.
rpcs.returnDescription: For return format see description in the routine at IN^DSICDPT2 in List[1

rpcs.name: DSIC DPT INP INFO BRIEF
rpcs.description: This will return certain data related to a patient's current admission.  ", 'The data returned and the order that it is returned is controlled by the FLAG parameter.  For more detailed description, see routine INQ^DSICDPT
rpcs.returnDescription: If problems or if the patient is not an inpatient, return -1^message  Else return a string, p1^p2^p3^p4^p5^...   where the data returned in each piece is controled by the FLAG parameter.

rpcs.name: DSIC ACTIVE USER
rpcs.description: This will determine if a user (DUZ value) is valid and is active.  Also, additionally screening logic can be passed in to validate a user.
rpcs.returnDescription: If user if found and is active, then return the user's DUZ value.", 'Else return -1^message

rpcs.name: DSIC FM LIST
rpcs.description: The RPC provides a wrapper around the LIST^DIC API.  It exposes more of the functionality of the API than the DSIC DDR LISTER RPC.  For a lookup value, return all entries starting from that lookup value and which collates after that lookup value.
rpcs.returnDescription: If problems return List[0

rpcs.name: DSIC ACTIVE USER LIST
rpcs.description: This will return a list of active users matching the lookup value.  You can pass in additional screening logic if so desired, such as security keys.  It is the programmers responsibility to ensure that the total length of the date returned in each return value does not exceed 250 characters.
rpcs.returnDescription: Data will be returned in ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"DILIST",n,0). p4 - sig block title          p8 = service  If problems are encountered, then return ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"DILIST",1,0) = -1^message If additional messages are provided then they will be at ^TMP("DSIC",$J,"DILIST",n,0) = message   for n=2,3,4,5,...  I

rpcs.name: DSIC FM FIND
rpcs.description: This provides a wrapper around the FIND^DIC API.  It exposes more of the functionality of the API to the RPC than the old DSIC DDR FINDER RPC.  For a lookup value, this RPC will return all matches.  It allows for input a multiple screening logic which would be ANDed together.
rpcs.returnDescription: if problems return one or more rows: etc., etc., .... List[0

rpcs.name: DENTV MULT PARAM
rpcs.description: This will return all instances for a parameter.  The Parameter should be multi-instance.  The difference between this call and the national XPAR call is that this call will return a value for every parameter-instance combination regardless of entity.  It will return the value for the entity of highest precedence.
rpcs.returnDescription: On error return List[0

rpcs.name: DENTV INPATIENT BEDSECTION
rpcs.description: This will return a string indicating whether or not the patient is a current inpatient.  If an inpatient, then also return, if possible, the PTF Specialty and Dental Bedsection associated with the current ward that the patient is lodged.
rpcs.returnDescription: RETURN: p1^p2^p3^p4^p5  where If not an inpatient, then this will only return 0 p1 = Boolean flag (1 or 0) indicating inpatient status p2 = PTF Specialty pointer p3 = PTF Specialty name p4 = DAS Bedsection pointer p5 = DAS Bedsection name  p2,p3,p4,p5 - only returned if patient is an inpatient On er

rpcs.name: DSIR ADD DOCUMENT
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to create an entry in file 19620.1 DSIR RELEASED DOCUMENT
rpcs.returnDescription: Return array consists of one node -1 ^ Error message 1 ^ IEN of new entry in File 19620.1

rpcs.name: DSIR GET DOCUMENTS

rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array IEN (file 19620.1 DSIR RELEASED DOCUMENT) ^ CERNER DOCUMENT Y/N ^ DOCUMENT TYPE ^ DOCUMENT IEN

rpcs.name: DSIR CLEANUP
rpcs.description: This RPC kills global nodes TMP("DSIROI",$J) and TMP("DSIRVAL",$J).
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is null.

rpcs.name: DSIR SET FOLLOWUP DATE
rpcs.description: Array Returned of Followup dates.
rpcs.returnDescription: Array Returned

rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array IEN of entry in file 19620 ROI INSTANCE

rpcs.name: DSIR CHECK PREV REQ
rpcs.description: This RPC checks for previous request for the current patient and requestor. It then returns an array of entries in file 19620 ROI INSTANCE.
rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array of: (1): Number of requests^ (2-n): Status^Date of request^Clerk of record for request^IEN

Note From^Prog. Note

rpcs.name: DSIR SET DOC DATES
rpcs.description: SAVES DATES FOR USE IN SCREENING CLINICAL DOCUMENTS FOR AN ROI REQUEST
rpcs.returnDescription: 1^ IF SUCCESSFUL -1^ERROR MESSAGE IF UNSUCCESSFUL

rpcs.name: DSIR REQUESTS SINCE DATE
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns one integer value which is -1 if there was an error, else returns the number of requests the patient has submitted since the date contained in VEJDDATE.

rpcs.name: DSIR RPT BY REQUESTOR

rpcs.returnDescription: Return Array ^ delimited 1. Division Name 2. ROI Clerk Name 3. Patient Name 4. Patient SSN 5. Type of Request 6. External Date Received 7. External Date Closed (If present)

rpcs.returnDescription: Return Value: 1.      -1 ^ Error Message 2.      Internal Code : Description (example O:OPEN)

rpcs.name: DSIR STATUS HISTORY
rpcs.description: Returns a Status History Array.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array

rpcs.name: DSIR GET REASONS FOR REQUEST
rpcs.description: Retrieve entries from file 19620.31 DSIR REQUEST REASON.
rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array IEN (file 19620.31) ^ Description

rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array IEN (file 19620.61) ^ Description

rpcs.description: This gets the entries from file 19620.51 DSIR AUTHORITY FOR REQUEST.
rpcs.returnDescription: IEN (file 19620.51) ^ Description

rpcs.description: This RPC is used to return an array of ROI INSTANCE (File 19620) entries. ', " STAT: Set of Codes: (Delimited by '^', defaults to *)", '     "*"  : Get all requests of a given type "O"  : Get only the open requests of this type "C"  : "          " closed "                " (-D,-G,-P) "P"  : "          " pending " "N"  : "          " other non-disclosure " (-NR,-RF,-ND,-NV,-RC,-NF,-NU) "E"  : "          " entered in error " "X"  : "          " cancelled "  "A"  : "          " appealed "
rpcs.returnDescription: IEN^Patient Name^External Date Entered^Status^Requestor^SSN^Priority^FMDT ^Exemptions^Expedite Reqstd^Expedite Granted^Clerk Name ^Opened by Clerk Name^Internal Opened Date^External Open Date ^Last Closed Clerk^Internal Last Closed Date^External Last Closed Date ^Last Reopen Clerk^Internal Last Reop

rpcs.name: DSIR ADD/EDIT ROI
rpcs.description: Create new or update existing ROI Instance File 19620.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return: AXY Successful Creation/Update: 1^IFN of file 19620 Unsuccessful Creation/Update: -1^Error Message

rpcs.name: DSIR REFORMAT REPORTS
rpcs.description: This RPC will take a given report array and resize the length to fit into 78 will be the remaining text with blank lines to fill to line 78 and lines 79 and 80 will be copies of lines 39 and 40.  If there are blank lines that can be removed from the bottom of the body to fit onto one page they will be removed so that the report will fit. a smaller print area.  It takes into account header and footer lines and resizes the main body of the report.  It may return more pages than the input array.  E
rpcs.returnDescription: This RPC returns an array comprised of entries in the incoming array, with blank lines removed or text lines moved to fit into the print length defined in parameter 1.

rpcs.name: DSIR ADD NONCOMP PAT
rpcs.description: Routine for adding or updating an non-computerized patient for ROI.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return values may be one of the following: This is returned if the SSN exists in file 19620.96, the calling program must determine if this is the same name entered.  1^NNNN;DSIR(19620.96,^NAME,PATIENT This is returned upon completion of a new entry being created or updated.  -3^Unable to create reco

rpcs.name: DSIR GET DOCUMENT TYPES
rpcs.description: Retrieve entries from file 19620.4 DSIR DOCUMENT TYPES
rpcs.returnDescription: Output: Array IEN (file 19620.4) ^ Description ^^ Availability

rpcs.description: This RPC is used to delete orphan requestors or repoint and delete that point to the requestor in parameter 1 to the requestor specified in parameter 2.  Input Parameters: 1. Duplicate/Delete Requestor - IEN to file 19620.12 (Required) 2. Repoint to Requestor - IEN to file 19620.12 (Optional)  Return Values: -1^Missing Requestor IEN! -2^Must Repoint Requestors in Use! duplicate requestors. -3^Invalid Requestor #1 -4^Invalid Requestor #2 IEN passed in parameter 1 - Orphan Deleted IEN passed in pa
rpcs.returnDescription: Return Values: -1^Missing Requestor IEN! -2^Must Repoint Requestors in Use! -3^Invalid Requestor #1 -4^Invalid Requestor #2 IEN passed in parameter 1 - Orphan Deleted IEN passed in parameter 2 - Duplicate repointed and deleted

rpcs.name: DSIR YEAR END FOIA REPORT
rpcs.description: This RPC will collect the data for the year end FOIA report for VA Form 0712.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return array will have entries with 2 pieces.  The first piece will be the Roman Numerial Part Number - Section Letter - Line Number - Block Letter, the second piece will contain the value to go in the specified block.  Example: IV-A-5701^N IV-A-5705^N V-A-1^7 V-B-3-A-10^0

rpcs.name: DSIR GET EXEMPTION 3 INFO
rpcs.description: This returns the values from fields 13.01 thru 13.1.  13.01 - 13.04 are Y/N values indicated by 1 or 0. 13.05 - 13.1 are free text upto 20 characters each.
rpcs.returnDescription: The array will consist of 5 elements. Items 1 thru 4 will be field values for fields 13.01 thru 13.04.( 1 or 0 )', "Item 5 will be the '^' delimited values of fields 13.05 thru 13.1.

rpcs.name: DSIR COMP ACCOUNTING SUMMARY
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list of all internal entry numbers from file 19620 for a specified patient.
rpcs.returnDescription: The array contains the internal numbers of the entries in file 19620.

rpcs.name: DSIR GET DIVISIONS
rpcs.description: Return available Divisions for reporting.  Must be in the ADIV cross reference on file 19620.
rpcs.returnDescription: The array will be the format: IEN ^ NAME.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST STRING
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes a string as input, adds some additional text to the string and returns the string back to the client.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the input parameter string with additional text added.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST GLOBAL ARRAY
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes an array as input, sets the array into a global array and returns the global array information back to the client.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the input array information to the client after being placed in a global array.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST GLOBAL NODE
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes a string as input, sets it as a global node and returns the global node back to the client.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the input string of information to the client after being placed in a global node.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST LOCAL ARRAY
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes an array as input, sets the array into a local array and returns the local array information back to the client.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the input array information returned to the client after being placed in a local array.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST WORD PROCESSING
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes no input parameters and returns a 'chunk' of text.", "This RPC is used to test whether VistALink properly transports 'chunks' of", 'text.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a 'chunk' of text. (Lincoln's Gettysburg Address)

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST PING
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes no input and returns a success message. This is the primary mechanism to test whether the client successfully connects to an M server using VistALink.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a message that the execution of the RPC has been successful.  The message is: Ping Successful!

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST RPC LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list of RPC names that begin with the characters indicated in the one input parameter.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a list of RPC names all beginning with the prefix indicated by the one input parameter.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST XML RESULTS
rpcs.description: This simple RPC takes no input parameters and returns a 'chunk' of data ", 'that is in XML format.  This RPC is used to test whether VistALink properly transports and parses application results in XML format.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a string in XML format.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST NOT IN CONTEXT
rpcs.description: This RPC should not be used and returns the empty string (null). ', "This RPC is used to test the 'not in RPC context' check. ", ' ', "For this reason, this RPC is specifically NOT in the 'XOBV VISTALINK", "TESTER' B-type option in the OPTION (#19) file.", ' This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the empty string.  See description on the use of this RPC.

rpcs.name: PSB CHECK IV
rpcs.description: RPC PSB CHECK IV - When given a patient's data file number(DFN), this ", 'process will return infusing IV information pertaining to the given DFN.  The information will be returned at the location presented by the "RESULTS" parameter.
rpcs.returnDescription: RESULTS is an output array which will contain informaion about infusing IVs.  The infusing IVs information will be relative to the given patient data file number (DFN).

rpcs.name: PSB VITALS
rpcs.description: Passes array of Vital entries - Temp,Pulse,Resp,BP,Pain in the last 7 days
rpcs.returnDescription: Results(0)=count Results(1)=Vital Type^Date/Time vital taken^DFN^vital value

rpcs.name: PSB VITAL MEAS FILE
rpcs.description: This RPC has been built specifically to process the filing of BCMA Parameters: Input  - DFN   (r) Pointer to the PATIENT (#2) file RATE  (r)  BCMA trigger event/transaction VTYPE (o) Pointer to GMRV VITAL TYPE FILE (#120.51) (default = Pain ["PN

rpcs.name: DSIC ACTIVE PERSON CLASS
rpcs.description: This will return a user's active person class for a given date.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return -1^message if problems or no active person class Else return p1^p2^p3^...^p8  where p1 = IEN to file 8932.1     p5 = Effective date p2 = Occupation             p6 = expiration date p3 = specialty              p7 = VA Code p4 = sub-specialty          p8 = specialty code

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ1 GETCNT
rpcs.description: Returns the count of sensitive patients left to evaluate after a main query iteration run.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ1 QRYSITR
rpcs.description: Similar to the main query iterator routine, except does not process the patients marked as sensitive, and saves them off without screening them.  Subsequent routines are called to manage the list and process those selected for reporting.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns PtSearched^RecordsFound^Iterator; use the Iterator returned for subsequent calls.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ1 QSITR
rpcs.description: Iterates through sensitive patients identified in a query tool report. Screens patients based on report criteria, and reports sensitive patients as necessary.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns PtSearched^RecordsFound^Iterator; use the Iterator returned in the subsequent call.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ1 GETSPT
rpcs.description: Gets sensitive patients from a query tool cohort, where the patients have not yet been screened.
rpcs.returnDescription: List of items in the format DFN^Patient Name^Sensitivity Level, sorted by patient name.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ1 GETSLN
rpcs.description: When a query executes, the sensitive patients are saved off before they are evaluated, along with the sensitive information gathered from the routine PTSEC^DGSEC4.  This RPC returns the sensitive message text for the given patient when this query attempted to query their record.
rpcs.returnDescription: Sensitive information text returned by PTSEC^DGSEC4 for this patient.

rpcs.name: ORRHCQ1 DELSEN
rpcs.description: Deletes the sensitive patient from the sensitive patient list generated for the currently executing query.  No further evaluation of the patients record will take place.
rpcs.returnDescription: 1=Success, -1=Failure

rpcs.name: ORRHCU DFLDMAP
rpcs.description: Returns a mapping from criteria categories to display field categories
rpcs.returnDescription: The returned array is a mapping in the form A=B, where A is the criteria category and B is the display field category.

rpcs.name: XHD DELETE PARAMETER CATEGORY
rpcs.description: This call deletes parameter categories from the M backing store. If the second parameter, DELKIDS is passed as "1" (boolean true by M programming convention), all descendents of the category in question will also be deleted.
rpcs.returnDescription: Error condition. Returns 0 if the category was successfully deleted, or 1^message if the call failed.

rpcs.name: XHD GET ALL PARAMETER DEFS
rpcs.description: Returns all Parameter Definitions.

rpcs.name: XHD GET CONFIGURATION
rpcs.description: Returns Configuration tree as XML in the following format:
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns XML in the following format:

rpcs.name: XHD GET MUTABLE CONFIGURATION
rpcs.description: Returns Configuration tree as XML in the following format:
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns XML in the following format:

rpcs.name: XHD GET PARAMETER DEF LIST
rpcs.description: Gets all parameter definitions as a list with IEN^NAME^DISPLAY NAME in each node of the return array.

rpcs.name: XHD GET PARAMETER DEFINITION
rpcs.description: This call returns an XML representation of a Parameter Definition.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is an XML representation of the Parameter Definition.

rpcs.name: XHD GET PARAMETER DEFINITIONS
rpcs.description: Gets parameter definitions as a list with IEN^NAME^DISPLAY NAME^ENTITYLIST in each node of the return array. This call returns a local array of SIZE elements in DIR direction from the initial Definition FROM.
rpcs.returnDescription: A local array of Parameter Definitions is returned with the following format:  ORRY(n)="IEN^PARAMETER DEFINITION NAME^DISPLAY NAME^ENTITY(1) IEN|ENTITY(1) NAME;ENTITY(2) IEN|ENTITY(2) NAME;...;ENTITY(n) IEN|ENTITY(n) NAME"

rpcs.name: XHD INSERT PARAMETER CATEGORY
rpcs.description: Inserts new parameter categories.
rpcs.returnDescription: This is an error flag, the first piece will be 0 if no error occurs during the filing process, or 1 if an error occurs, followed by an explanatory message.

rpcs.name: XHD PUT PARAMETER
rpcs.description: Call PUT^XPAR or PUTWP^XPAR to file a given parameter.
rpcs.returnDescription: An Error condition.

rpcs.name: XHD REMOVE PARAMETER CATEGORY
rpcs.description: This call removes parameter categories from their parents (i.e., "prunes" the branch of which the the category is the root). It does NOT delete the Category or its descendents. Use the ORR DELETE PARAMETER CATEGORY to delete a given categor and all its descendents.
rpcs.returnDescription: Error condition. Returns 0 if the category was successfully removed from its parent, or 1^message if the call failed.

rpcs.name: XHD UPDATE PARAMETER CATEGORY
rpcs.description: This call updates ParameterCategories.

rpcs.description: This returns the list of perspective id's that should be visible for the", 'current user.

rpcs.name: XHDX VERSRV

rpcs.name: XHD TEST INPUT LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC returns the list that it's been passed. It's for test purposes ", 'only.

rpcs.name: DENTV FEE BASIS ADD
rpcs.description: This RPC adds, or edits a record in the Dental Fee Basis file (#228.5).
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns 1 if successful, else returns -1^message.

rpcs.name: DENTV FEE BASIS LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC gets a list of DENTAL FEE BASIS EXTRACT (#228.5) records for a specified patient.  The input is a single value, patient id (DFN).
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array containing the internal entry number (IEN) appended to the beginning of the zero node of the DENTAL FEE BASIS EXTRACT file, in the format:  ien;txnUID^create dt^create person^patient^facility^ category^date authorized^cost^^^^

rpcs.name: DENTV TP FILE PSR
rpcs.description: This RPC adds (or updates) a record to the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (#228.2).  The only record type supported by this RPC is the PSR type, where the TYPE field (.29) = 3.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns ien^message if successful, else returns -1^error message if not.

rpcs.name: DENTV TP FILE PERIO
rpcs.description: This RPC adds (or updates) a reocrd to the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (#228.2).  The only record type supported by this RPC is the Perio type, where the TYPE field (.29) = 2.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns ien^message if successful, else -1^error message.

rpcs.name: DENTV TP FILE HNC
rpcs.description: This RPC adds, updates or marks as deleted, records to the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (#228.2).  The only record type supported by this RPC is the Head and Neck type, where the TYPE field (.29)=4.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns an array of messages based on the number of Head&Neck transactions that were sent, in the format: return(n)=ien^message  if successful, or return(n)=-1^error message   if not successful.

rpcs.name: DENTV TP ADD ENCOUNTER
rpcs.description: This will add a new record to the dental encounter file, 228.1
rpcs.returnDescription: If successfully add a new record, return ien to file 228.1^message Else return -1^message  If complications were also added, a seperate message will be returned regarding its status as well.

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET ADA TABLE
rpcs.description: This returns the ADA table (file 228) for the Discus Treatment Planning system.
rpcs.returnDescription: return ^TMP("DENT",$J,cpt_code) = p1^p2^...^p13 p7 = ranged (boolean)  p14 = tooth related (boolean) P15 = diagnosis description boolean in treatment planning is -1 for true, 0 for false  header records are also returned: ^TMP("DENT",$J,cpt_header_code) = cpt code^cpt description ^TMP("DENT",$J,cpt_

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET PROVIDERS
rpcs.description: This will return a list of all providers who have provided service to this patient.  Each provider will have a flag indicating whether or not the provider is current.
rpcs.returnDescription: if problems, return DENT(1) = -1^message else return DENT(n) = DUZ^Initials^IsCurrent^Name where n = 1,2,3,4,... IsCurrent is Boolean (-1 is true, 0 is false)

rpcs.name: DENTV TP FILE TOOTH NOTE
rpcs.description: This will allow you to add, update, or delete a record in file 228.6
rpcs.returnDescription: If action is successful, RET(n)= 1^message Else RET(n)= -1^message

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET TOOTH NOTES
rpcs.description: This returns all the tooth notes for all dates for a patient.  The data will be sorted by tooth number and then by reverse date.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return: global array List[n

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET HNC
rpcs.description: Returns all of the Head&Neck records from the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (#228.2) for a particular patient.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a global array List[n

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET PSR
rpcs.description: Returns all of the PSR type records from the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (#228.2) for a particular patient.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a global array List[n

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET PERIO
rpcs.description: Returns all of the Perio type records from the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (#228.2) for a particular patient.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a global array List[n

rpcs.name: DENTV TP FILE TRANSACTIONS
rpcs.description: This will file transaction data types to file 228.2.  It will also create the DAS record (#221) if appropriate.  It will also file data to PCE if appropriate.
rpcs.returnDescription: If all filing is successful, then the return array will contain: If unsuccessful, then return -1^error messages The first line returned will start with -1^ Subsequent message lines may or may not start with -1^ 1^DAS record# <number> created 1^<number> DES transactions successfully filed 1^PCE data

rpcs.name: DENTV DELETE TRANSACTIONS
rpcs.description: This RPC deletes the dental encounter from the DENTAL HISTORY file (#228.1) and dental transactions from the DENTAL TREATMENT PLAN/EXAM file (#228.2) for types=PSR, PerioExam and Head&Neck if the Transaction(s) for type=Transaction fails to file.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns  1^Records deleted, or -1^error message if the IEN lookup fails.

rpcs.name: DENTV TP GET TRANSACTIONS
rpcs.description: Returns all of the Transaction type records from the Treatment Plan Transaction/Exam file (228.2) for a particular patient..
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns a global array List[n

rpcs.name: DSIR FOIA VALIDATION
rpcs.description: This RPC will return a global array of all records thkat were looked at for the year end FOIA report.  It will contain the internal request number and patient name/FOIA indicator, and a one for any block on the report that the given request was included on.  It also contains all record that, during the report period, were entered in error or of the type of request/requestor type of an excluded state reporting agency.

DVBAB1 = IEN in 396.17 to copy DVBAB2 = IEN in patient file  If DVBAB2 is null, the copied form will be filed under the same patient it previously belonged to.

If there is a second parameter, then we are "setting" the providers into the DENTAL PATIENT file and we will return DFN^Successful if we were able to file

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST MULTIPLE SUBSCRIPTS
rpcs.description: This RPC receives an array set up by a client. The client code indicates that the array should be presented to the RPC as multiple M subscripts (ex: DATA("TEXT",1,0) and not the usual DATA(1).  It sets the array into a local array and returns the local array information back to the client.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed as part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns the input array information returned to the client after being placed in a local array.

rpcs.name: XOBV TEST MULT ARRAY PARAMS
rpcs.description: This RPC receives 3 arrays set up by a client. This tests the capability of VistaLink to support multiple array-type RPC parameters.  It sets each of the three arrays into a local array, sequentially, and returns the local array back to the client.  This RPC is used as part of the test and learning application distributed part of VistALink.
rpcs.returnDescription: The input array information returned to the client after being placed in a local array.

rpcs.name: DSIV REPORT QUEUE

rpcs.name: DSIV GET EIV REPORT
rpcs.description: Returns data from the IIV RESPONSE file (365) based on a buffer ien.

If an eIV error exists, the full IIV RESPONSE (#365.15) file error data is returned following an error indicator line: $$ERROR$$.

rpcs.name: DSIV REPORT DATA
rpcs.description: Retrieves data from the audit file 19625 for billing report.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns -1^error message or RET(n) p1^p2^p3^p4... RET(n)=$END$ at end of list where p1 = ien of record and p2-pn are requested fields

If one of the following fields is updated in the parent or the child of a group that has only one image, then the changes are replicated to the child or parent respectively: SHORT DESCRIPTION (10), TYPE INDEX (42), PROC/EVENT INDEX (43), SPEC/SUBSPEC INDEX (44), ORIGIN INDEX (45), CREATION DATE (110), CONTROLLED IMAGE (112), STATUS (113), and STATUS REASON (113.3).

rpcs.returnDescription: Zero value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: MAGG REASON GET PROPERTIES

rpcs.returnDescription: Zero value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: MAGG IMAGE STATISTICS

Counts of images captured in the provided date range grouped by users and image status codes.
rpcs.returnDescription: Zero value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: MAGG IMAGE LOCK

rpcs.returnDescription: Zero value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.returnDescription: Zero value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: RAMAG ORDER CANCEL
rpcs.description: The RAMAG ORDER CANCEL remote procedure cancels/holds the Radiology order and sends all required notifications.  NOTE: If there are active cases in the RAD/NUC MED PATIENT file (#70) associated with an order, this remote procedure neither cancels nor holds the order and returns the error code -42.
rpcs.returnDescription: A negative value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: RAMAG EXAM CANCEL
rpcs.description: The RAMAG EXAM CANCEL remote procedure cancels the Radiology exam(s) and sends all required notifications.  If all exams that reference the same order/request are canceled, this function can also cancel/hold the order (if the appropriate parameters are provided).
rpcs.returnDescription: A negative value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: RAMAG EXAM COMPLETE
rpcs.description: The RAMAG EXAM COMPLETE remote procedure completes the exam. It also sends required HL7 messages, sends changed order control "XX" to CPRS, but does not send VistA alerts regarding the exam status change.
rpcs.returnDescription: A negative value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: RAMAG EXAMINED
rpcs.description: The RAMAG EXAMINED remote procedure updates the status of the case (the procedure has been performed) and creates the stub report. It also sends required HL7 messages, sends changed order control "XX" to CPRS, but does not send VistA alerts regarding the exam status change.
rpcs.returnDescription: A negative value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: RAMAG EXAM STATUS REQUIREMENTS
rpcs.description: The RAMAG EXAM STATUS REQUIREMENTS remote procedure returns a descriptor that indicates conditions that should be met in order to successfully perform an action on an exam/case record.  These conditions are defined by the sites and stored in the EXAMINATION STATUS file (#72). See the .1 and .5 nodes of the data dictionary of the file #74 for more details.
rpcs.returnDescription: A negative value of the first '^'-piece of the Results[0

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM ADD CAMERA EQUIP RM
rpcs.description: Check the OUTSIDE STUDY Camera Equipment Room for the IMAGING LOCATION. If it is not present, add it.
rpcs.returnDescription: Success/Error results

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET DFN
rpcs.description: Given an ICN, it returns the DFN.
rpcs.returnDescription: DFN

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET HOSP LOCATION
rpcs.description: Return a list of matching hospital locations
rpcs.returnDescription: List of matching hospital locations in "ien^name" format.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD CAMERA
rpcs.description: Used to find an entry in file CAMERA/EQUIP/RM (#78.6)
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns: RESULTS(1)= -1 (entry not found) or 1 (entry found) RESULTS(2)= ien^name of entry^description of entry.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD CPT MOD
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to return a valid entry in the CPT MODIFIER file (#81.3).
rpcs.returnDescription: RESULTS(1)= -1 (entry not found) or 1 (entry found) RESULTS(2)= ien^CPT name

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD DX CODE
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to lookup an entry in file DIAGNOSTIC CODES (78.3).
rpcs.returnDescription: RESULTS(1)= -1 (entry not found) or 1 (entry found) RESULTS(2)= ien^diagnostic name

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD FILM
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to find an entry in the FILM SIZE file (#78.4).
rpcs.returnDescription: RESULTS(1)= -1 (entry not found) or 1 (entry found) RESULTS(2)= ien^film size name

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD INFO BY ACN
rpcs.description: This RPC takes the Accession Number and returns the first radiology study that matches it.  This RPC uses $$ACCFIND^RAAPI() to get this information.
rpcs.returnDescription: The results will be either: If successful:    1^RADFN_^_RADTI_^_RACNI_^_IMAGLOCN If unsuccessful: -1^"invalid site accession number format"^accession # or -2^"invalid accession number format"^accession # or -3^"no data associated with this accession number"^accession #

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD ORDERS
rpcs.description: Return a list of radiology orders for a patient
rpcs.returnDescription: List of radiogy orders for the patient.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET RAD PERSON
rpcs.description: Returns list of names from the NEW PERSON file (#200) with a Rad/Nuc class.
rpcs.returnDescription: Unsuccessful lookup returns: RESULTS(1)="-1^No provider matching input. RESULTS(1)="-2,More than "_MAXNR_" matches; please specify RESULTS(1)=RESULTS(1)_" more characters in the name. Successful lookup returns: RESULTS(1)= 1 RESULTS(2)= ien^name

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM GET USERNAME
rpcs.description: Returns the name of the user.
rpcs.returnDescription: Name of user from NEW PERSON file (#200).

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM IMPORTER CHECK UIDS
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to lookup DICOM UIDs before importing images.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return a list that matched the UID provided.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM IMPORTER DELETE
rpcs.description: This RPC is used by the DICOM Gateway to delete an entry in file IMPORTABLE DICOM OBJECTS (#2006.5752).  The entry to be deleted must match the Hostname, OLDPATH (Gateway), and the image UID.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM IMPORTER LOOKUP
rpcs.description: Return information about the image.
rpcs.returnDescription: Returns patient demographics, case number, and study date, procedure and procedure modifier.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM RADIOLOGY MODIFIERS
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a listing of entries from the PROCEDURES MODIFIER file (#71.2) sorted by Radiology' Imaging Type.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM RADIOLOGY PROCEDURES
rpcs.description: This RPC returns a list of Radiology Procedures for 'no-credit' Imaging", 'locations within a given division.', "If the division does not have any 'no-credit' Imaging locations defined,", 'the results will return an error message indicating the problem.

rpcs.name: MAG DICOM SET IMAGING LOCATION
rpcs.description: Set imaging location in radiology order file (#75.1).
rpcs.returnDescription: Status: positive values are good, errors are negative.

rpcs.name: EC RECENT VISITS
rpcs.description: Returns the 20 most recent visits/appointments for a selected patient for the selected location.
rpcs.returnDescription: Return parameter is an array containing: Piece 1 - Date of visit, internal fileman date Piece 2 - Date of Visit (readable) and  Clinic Name Piece 3 - Date of Visit (readable) Piece 4 - Clinic Name

rpcs.description: Calls database server at GETS^DIQ

rpcs.name: NHIN GET VISTA DATA
rpcs.description: This RPC retrieves the requested data from VistA, and returns it in ^TMP($J,"NHINV",n) as XML.
rpcs.returnDescription: Text array formatted as XML

rpcs.name: EDPCTRL RPC
rpcs.description: This RPC acts as the "front controller" for the EDIS Tracking Application. It accepts requests that are initially passed into a web server.  The RPC uses the parameters that are passed in to determine which command to execute.  The returned data is formatted as XML.  The XML structure varies based a what data were requested.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return results are formatted as an XML document.

rpcs.name: EDPCBRD RPC
rpcs.description: This RPC acts as the "front controller" for the EDIS Display Board.  It accepts requests that are initially passed into a web server.  The RPC uses the parameters that are passed in to determine which command to execute.  The returned data is formatted as XML.  The XML structure varies based a what data were requested.  Since the Display Board runs in a "kiosk" mode, this RPC allows proxy user access.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return results are formatted as an XML document.

rpcs.name: ROR GET M VERSION
rpcs.description: Used by CCR to return an agreed-upon version number to the GUI application.  The GUI will analyze the version number and display a message if the client and server are out of sync.

rpcs.name: VAFC AA UPDATE
rpcs.description: This remote procedure call will update assigning authority passed in by MPI to VAFC ASSIGNING AUTHORITY file #391.92.

rpcs.name: MPI EVENT LIST
rpcs.description: This RPC is used to return the list of all LINK and DO NOT LINK records associated with the ICN list passed as input.  The DO NOT LINK list will be derived from the records in the MPI DO NOT LINK file (#985.28) and pulled using the GET^MPIDNL API. The LINK list will be built from PRIMARY ICN values associated with any of the ICNs in the input list.
rpcs.returnDescription: The return value is a list of records that the given record should not be and/or  requested ICN/VPID|SourceSystemID|SourceAssigningAuthority|IDType ^DO NOT LINK^record ID^SourceSystemID^SourceAssigningAuthority^IDType  Example:  RETURN(1)=123456789V123456|200M|USVHA|NI|T ^LINK^1111111111V111111|200M

rpcs.name: MDCP UPDATE MESSAGE REASON
